<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by `cargo xtask gen-docs` ‚Äî do not edit manually -->

Anodizer uses `.anodizer.yaml` (or `.anodizer.toml`) in your project root.

## Top-Level Fields

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `after` | HooksConfig | ‚Äî | Hooks run after the release pipeline completes. |
| `announce` | AnnounceConfig | ‚Äî | Announcement configuration (Slack, Discord, email, etc.). |
| `appimages` | list of AppImageConfig | `[]` | AppImage configurations. Each entry bundles a built Linux binary plus its desktop integration into a single self-contained `.AppImage` via linuxdeploy. |
| `artifactories` | list of ArtifactoryConfig | ‚Äî | Artifactory upload configurations. |
| `attestations` | AttestationConfig | ‚Äî | SLSA build-provenance / attestation configuration for binaries and archives. In the default `subjects` mode, anodizer writes a subjects manifest for `actions/attest-build-provenance`; in `emit` mode it generates and signs a self-contained in-toto SLSA provenance statement. When omitted (or `enabled: false`), the attestation stage is a no-op. |
| `aur_sources` | list of AurSourceConfig | ‚Äî | AUR source package publishing configurations (source-only PKGBUILD, not -bin). |
| `before` | HooksConfig | ‚Äî | Hooks run before the release pipeline starts. |
| `before_publish` | HooksConfig | ‚Äî | Hooks run after build/archive/sign/sbom/checksum complete but immediately before the publish phase dispatches any publisher.

Use cases: smoke-test artifacts against the staged dist tree, run external validators (antivirus, vulnerability scanners), stage external state, or abort the release before any publisher writes to a registry.

A non-zero exit code from any hook aborts the release before publish runs. Hooks fire in declared order. Use `--skip=before-publish` to bypass. |
| `binary_signs` | list of SignConfig | `[]` | Binary-specific signing configs (same shape as `signs` but only for binary artifacts). The `artifacts` field on each entry is constrained at parse time to `binary` / `none` (or omitted) ‚Äî a broader filter on `binary_signs` would silently match nothing because the loop only iterates Binary artifacts. Constraint lives in `deserialize_binary_signs`. |
| `changelog` | ChangelogConfig | ‚Äî | Changelog generation configuration. |
| `cloudsmiths` | list of CloudSmithConfig | ‚Äî | CloudSmith publisher configurations. |
| `crates` | list of CrateConfig | `[]` | List of crates in this project. |
| `defaults` | Defaults | ‚Äî | Default values applied to all crates unless overridden. |
| `dist` | string | `./dist` | Output directory for build artifacts (default: ./dist). |
| `docker_signs` | list of DockerSignConfig | ‚Äî | Docker image signing configurations. |
| `dockerhub` | list of DockerHubConfig | ‚Äî | DockerHub description sync configurations. |
| `env` | list of string | ‚Äî | Environment variables available to all template expressions.

List of `KEY=VALUE` strings: `env: ["MY_VAR=hello", "DEPLOY_ENV=staging"]`. Order is preserved so chained env applications (sign + sbom + notarize) see entries in declared order. Values are rendered through the template engine before being set, so expressions like `{{ Tag }}` or `{{ Date }}` are expanded. |
| `env_files` | EnvFilesConfig | ‚Äî | Environment file configuration. Accepts either: - A list of `.env` file paths: `[".env", ".release.env"]` - A struct with token file paths: `{ github_token: "~/.config/goreleaser/github_token" }` |
| `force_token` | ForceTokenKind | ‚Äî | Force a specific token type for authentication. When set, overrides automatic token detection from environment variables. |
| `gemfury` | list of GemFuryConfig | ‚Äî | GemFury (fury.io) deb/rpm/apk publishing configurations. Mirrors The `gemfury:` block. The legacy spelling `furies:` is accepted via serde alias; a one-time deprecation warning is emitted by [`warn_on_legacy_furies_alias`]. |
| `git` | GitConfig | ‚Äî | Git-level tag discovery and sorting settings. |
| `gitea_urls` | GiteaUrlsConfig | ‚Äî | Custom Gitea API/download URLs for self-hosted Gitea installations. |
| `github_urls` | GitHubUrlsConfig | ‚Äî | Custom GitHub API/upload/download URLs for GitHub Enterprise installations. |
| `gitlab_urls` | GitLabUrlsConfig | ‚Äî | Custom GitLab API/download URLs for self-hosted GitLab installations. |
| `homebrew_casks` | list of HomebrewCaskConfig | ‚Äî | Top-level Homebrew Cask configurations. `homebrew_casks` is a top-level array with its own repository, commit_author, directory, skip_upload, hooks, dependencies, conflicts, completions, manpages, structured uninstall/zap, etc. |
| `includes` | list of IncludeSpec | ‚Äî | Additional config files to merge into this config. Supports plain string paths, `from_file:` for structured file paths, and `from_url:` for fetching configs from URLs with optional headers. |
| `makeselfs` | list of MakeselfConfig | `[]` | Makeself self-extracting archive configurations. |
| `mcp` | McpConfig | `{"name":null,"title":null,"description":null,"homepage":null,"packages":[],"transports":[],"skip":null,"repository":{"url":"","source":"","id":"","subfolder":""},"auth":{"type":"none"},"registry":null,"if":null}` | MCP (Model Context Protocol) server registry publishing configuration. When `name` is empty (the default), the publisher is skipped. The `mcp:` publisher block. |
| `metadata` | MetadataConfig | ‚Äî | Project metadata configuration (applied to metadata.json output files). |
| `milestones` | list of MilestoneConfig | ‚Äî | Milestone closing configurations. |
| `monorepo` | MonorepoConfig | ‚Äî | Monorepo configuration. When configured, tag discovery filters by tag_prefix and the working directory is scoped to dir. |
| `nightly` | NightlyConfig | ‚Äî | Nightly release configuration. |
| `notarize` | NotarizeConfig | ‚Äî | macOS code signing and notarization configuration. |
| `npms` | list of NpmConfig | ‚Äî | NPM package registry publishing configurations. One entry per published package. In the default `optional-deps` mode anodizer emits npm's native per-platform packages (biome / git-cliff pattern); in `postinstall` mode it emits a download shim (the `npms:` parity). |
| `partial` | PartialConfig | ‚Äî | Partial/split build configuration for fan-out CI pipelines. |
| `project_name` | string | ‚Äî | Human-readable project name used in templates and release titles. |
| `publishers` | list of PublisherConfig | ‚Äî | Generic artifact publisher configurations. |
| `release` | ReleaseConfig | ‚Äî | GitHub release configuration shared by all crates. |
| `report_sizes` | bool | ‚Äî | When true, log artifact file sizes after building. |
| `retry` | RetryConfig | ‚Äî | Top-level retry configuration applied to network-bound operations (announcers, git providers, HTTP uploads, docker pipes). When omitted, `RetryConfig::default()` is used (10 attempts, 10s base, 5m cap ‚Äî the project-level retry policy). |
| `sboms` | list of SbomConfig | `[]` | Software bill of materials (SBOM) generation configurations. |
| `schemastore` | SchemastoreConfig | `{"repository":null,"commit_author":null,"versioned":null,"skip":null,"if":null,"schemas":[]}` | SchemaStore publisher. Registers the project's JSON Schema(s) on SchemaStore at release time. When `schemas` is empty (the default), the publisher is skipped. The `schemastore:` publisher block. |
| `signs` | list of SignConfig | `[]` | Signing configurations for binaries, archives, and checksums. |
| `snapshot` | SnapshotConfig | ‚Äî | Snapshot release configuration (local/non-tag builds). |
| `source` | SourceConfig | ‚Äî | Source archive configuration. |
| `srpms` | SrpmConfig | ‚Äî | Source RPM configuration. Renamed from `srpm:` (singular) for spelling parity with `Defaults.srpms` and the rest of the plural-name packaging fields. The `srpm:` spelling is still accepted via serde alias for back-compat. |
| `tag` | TagConfig | ‚Äî | Automatic semantic version tagging configuration. |
| `template_files` | list of TemplateFileConfig | ‚Äî | Template files to render and include as release artifacts. File contents are processed through the template engine. |
| `uploads` | list of UploadConfig | ‚Äî | Generic HTTP upload configurations. |
| `upx` | list of UpxConfig | `[]` | UPX binary compression configurations. |
| `variables` | map | ‚Äî | Custom template variables accessible as `{{ Var.<key> }}` in templates. Provides a way to define reusable values, especially useful with config includes.

Stored as a `BTreeMap` so rendering iterates in deterministic (sorted) key order ‚Äî without this guarantee, a value that references another variable (`b: "{{ Var.a }}_v2"`) could render before its dependency on a different process / host. The current resolver is single-pass (one render per value), so cross-variable references only resolve when the referenced key sorts earlier. |
| `verify_release` | VerifyReleaseConfig | `{"enabled":false,"assert_assets":true,"install_smoke":null}` | Opt-in post-release verification gate. Runs LAST (after the release is created and every publisher has run) and REPORTS post-publish defects ‚Äî missing assets, failed install smoke-tests, glibc-ceiling violations. Because it runs after the irreversible publish, a failure exits non-zero to flag CI but never undoes the release. Off unless `verify_release.enabled: true`. |
| `version` | integer | ‚Äî | Schema version. Currently supports 1 (implicit default) and 2. |
| `version_files` | list of string | ‚Äî | Repo-committed files that embed the release version outside `Cargo.toml` (e.g. a Helm `Chart.yaml`, an install doc, a README badge), given as repo-root-relative path strings. At `tag` time each listed file has its occurrences of the old version rewritten to the new version ‚Äî both the bare (`0.1.0`) and `v`-prefixed (`v0.1.0`) forms, word-boundary anchored ‚Äî and is staged into the same bump commit as `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock`, so these files never drift from the tag.

```yaml version_files: - charts/cfgd/Chart.yaml - docs/installation.md ``` |
| `workspaces` | list of WorkspaceConfig | ‚Äî | Independent workspace roots in a monorepo. |

## `after`
Top-level lifecycle hooks for `before` and `after` blocks. Each block carries a list of hook commands that run around the entire pipeline (not individual stages).

The canonical key is `hooks:` for both `before:` and `after:` to the conventional spelling. The `post:` spelling is accepted as a serde alias on `hooks` for back-compat with the previous anodizer spelling; users with `after: { post: [...] }` keep working and a deprecation warning is logged when both spellings appear in the same block (see [`HooksConfig::merge_hook_aliases`]).
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `hooks` | list of HookEntry | ‚Äî | Commands to run when the block fires. The wire format accepts either `hooks:` (canonical) or the legacy `post:` spelling; both fold into this field at parse time. |
| `post` | list of HookEntry | ‚Äî | Legacy alias for `hooks:` (anodizer pre-v0.4). Always `None` after parsing ‚Äî `merge_hook_aliases` collapses it into `hooks`. Present on the struct only because `Deserialize` writes through it before the fold step. |

| `gate_on` | AnnounceGate | `required_publishers` | Selects when AnnounceStage runs vs. skips based on the `PublishReport` written by PublishStage/BlobStage. Default is `required_publishers` (announce only if every required publisher succeeded). See [`AnnounceGate`] for the other variants. |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the entire announce stage is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. The `announce.if:`. Distinct from `skip:` (always-skip predicate) ‚Äî both surfaces are documented. |
| `linkedin` | LinkedInAnnounce | ‚Äî | LinkedIn announcement configuration. |
| `mastodon` | MastodonAnnounce | ‚Äî | Mastodon announcement configuration. |
| `mattermost` | MattermostAnnounce | ‚Äî | Mattermost announcement configuration. |
| `opencollective` | OpenCollectiveAnnounce | ‚Äî | OpenCollective announcement configuration. |
| `reddit` | RedditAnnounce | ‚Äî | Reddit announcement configuration. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Template-conditional skip: if rendered to "true", skip the entire announce stage. |
| `slack` | SlackAnnounce | ‚Äî | Slack announcement configuration. |
| `teams` | TeamsAnnounce | ‚Äî | Microsoft Teams announcement configuration. |
| `telegram` | TelegramAnnounce | ‚Äî | Telegram announcement configuration. |
| `twitter` | TwitterAnnounce | ‚Äî | Twitter/X announcement configuration. |
| `webhook` | WebhookConfig | ‚Äî | Generic webhook announcement configuration. |

## `appimages`
AppImage packaging configuration.

Drives the [AppImage](https://appimage.org/) stage, which bundles a built Linux binary plus its desktop integration (a `.desktop` entry + icon) into a single self-contained, runnable `.AppImage` file via [`linuxdeploy`](https://github.com/linuxdeploy/linuxdeploy)'s `appimage` output plugin. One `.AppImage` is produced per matching Linux target so a multi-arch build yields distinct, non-colliding outputs.

YAML: ```yaml appimages: - id: helix ids: [helix-bin] desktop: contrib/Helix.desktop icon: contrib/helix.png appdir_extra: - src: runtime/ dst: usr/lib/helix/runtime update_information: "gh-releases-zsync|helix-editor|helix|latest|helix-*.AppImage.zsync" runtime_harvest: command: "{{ ArtifactPath }} --populate-runtime {{ HarvestDir }}" dir: runtime/ ```
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `appdir_extra` | list of AppImageExtra | ‚Äî | Extra files / directories copied into the AppDir before linuxdeploy runs (e.g. a harvested `runtime/` tree). Each entry's `dst` is interpreted relative to the AppDir root. |
| `arch` | list of string | ‚Äî | Target architecture filter. When omitted, every architecture in the build matrix produces its own `.AppImage`. |
| `desktop` | string | ‚Äî | Path to the `.desktop` entry file (template). Required ‚Äî linuxdeploy will not assemble an AppImage without a desktop file. |
| `extra_args` | list of string | ‚Äî | Extra arguments appended to the linuxdeploy command line. |
| `filename` | string | ‚Äî | Output filename template (default includes project, version, os, arch). The `.AppImage` extension is appended automatically when absent. |
| `icon` | string | ‚Äî | Path to the application icon (template). Required. |
| `id` | string | ‚Äî | Unique identifier for this AppImage config (default: "default"). |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build IDs filter: only bundle binaries whose `id` is in this list. When omitted, every Linux binary in the build matrix is eligible. |
| `name` | string | ‚Äî | Application name passed to linuxdeploy via the `APP` env var and used as the AppDir basename. Defaults to the project name. |
| `os` | list of string | ‚Äî | Target OS filter (default: ["linux"]). AppImage is a Linux-only format. |
| `runtime_harvest` | RuntimeHarvest | ‚Äî | Runtime-asset harvest hook: run the freshly-built binary ONCE on the host to populate a directory, then bundle that directory into the AppDir. The harvested data is architecture-independent (grammars, themes, queries), so it is produced once on the host-native binary and reused for every target's AppImage. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip this config. Accepts bool or template string. |
| `update_information` | string | ‚Äî | zsync delta-update metadata embedded in the AppImage, passed to linuxdeploy via the `UPDATE_INFORMATION` env var. When omitted, the AppImage carries no update information and `UPDATE_INFORMATION` is left unset (matching linuxdeploy's default). |

## `artifactories`
Artifactory upload configuration. Uploads artifacts to JFrog Artifactory repositories.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `checksum` | bool | ‚Äî | Include checksums in uploaded artifacts. |
| `checksum_header` | string | ‚Äî | Header name used for checksum verification (e.g. `X-Checksum-Sha256`). |
| `client_x509_cert` | string | ‚Äî | Path to client X.509 certificate for mTLS authentication. |
| `client_x509_key` | string | ‚Äî | Path to client X.509 private key for mTLS authentication. |
| `custom_artifact_name` | bool | ‚Äî | Use custom artifact naming instead of default. |
| `custom_headers` | map | ‚Äî | Custom HTTP headers sent with each upload request. |
| `extra_files` | list of ExtraFileSpec | ‚Äî | Extra files to upload alongside build artifacts. |
| `extra_files_only` | bool | ‚Äî | When true, upload only extra_files (skip normal artifacts). |
| `exts` | list of string | ‚Äî | File extension filter: only upload artifacts matching these extensions. |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build IDs filter: only upload artifacts from builds whose `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the artifactory publisher is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. The `artifactories[].if:`. |
| `meta` | bool | ‚Äî | Include metadata artifacts in uploaded artifacts. |
| `method` | string | ‚Äî | HTTP method to use for uploads (default: "PUT"). |
| `mode` | string | ‚Äî | Upload mode: "archive" (upload archives) or "binary" (upload binaries). |
| `name` | string | ‚Äî | Human-readable name for this publisher (used in logs). |
| `password` | string | ‚Äî | Artifactory password or API key (or env var reference). |
| `required` | bool | ‚Äî | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.

Default: `false` ‚Äî a failure here is logged but does not abort the release. Set to `true` to fail the release on any error. |
| `signature` | bool | ‚Äî | Include signatures in uploaded artifacts. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Template-conditional skip: if rendered result is `"true"`, skip this publisher. |
| `target` | string | ‚Äî | Target URL template for uploads (supports template variables). |
| `trusted_certificates` | string | ‚Äî | PEM-encoded trusted CA certificates for TLS verification. Appended to the system certificate pool. |
| `username` | string | ‚Äî | Artifactory username for authentication. |

## `attestations`
SLSA build-provenance / attestation configuration for binaries and archives.

Two modes select how anodizer participates in attestation:

- [`AttestationMode::Subjects`] (the default) emits a **subjects manifest** (`dist/attestation-subjects.json`) that `anodizer-action` feeds to GitHub's `actions/attest-build-provenance`. anodizer does NOT mint a GitHub-trusted attestation itself in this mode ‚Äî the Action's OIDC identity does. This is the path fd / biome / gping use. - [`AttestationMode::Emit`] generates a self-contained in-toto v1 statement carrying an SLSA provenance v1 predicate over the selected artifacts, writes it as a release asset (`attestation.intoto.jsonl`), and lets the existing `signs:` stage sign it (keyed, not OIDC). This is for users who can't run the Action (the `--with-provenance` toggle).

YAML: ```yaml attestations: enabled: true mode: subjects          # or: emit ; default = subjects artifacts: [archive, binary, checksum] ```
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `artifacts` | list of AttestationArtifactKind | ‚Äî | Which produced-artifact kinds to attest. Each entry selects a KIND (`archive`, `binary`, `checksum`); the concrete subject set (filenames + sha256) is DERIVED from the artifacts anodizer already produced.

Defaults to `[archive, binary, checksum]` when omitted. |
| `enabled` | bool | `false` | Enable attestation. When false (the default), the stage is a no-op. |
| `mode` | AttestationMode | ‚Äî | Participation mode: `subjects` (default) writes a manifest for `actions/attest-build-provenance`; `emit` generates and signs an in-toto SLSA provenance statement as a release asset. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip the attestation stage. Accepts a bool or a template string. |

## `aur_sources`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `amd64_variant` | Amd64Variant | ‚Äî | `x86_64` micro-architecture variant ‚Äî `v1` (baseline), `v2`, `v3` (AVX2), or `v4`. Constrained to a typed enum because AUR source pkgs build from the upstream tarball (no binary artifacts to filter), so the value's only role is as the `Amd64` template var consumed by `prepare:` / `build:` / `package:` script bodies ‚Äî typos must fail at parse time, not silently render an invalid string into the PKGBUILD. When unset, defaults to `v1` at template-render time. |
| `arches` | list of string | ‚Äî | Explicit architecture list (default: auto-detect from artifacts). |
| `backup` | list of string | ‚Äî | Backup files to preserve on upgrade. |
| `build` | string | ‚Äî | Custom `build()` function body for PKGBUILD. |
| `commit_author` | CommitAuthorConfig | ‚Äî | Commit author with optional signing. |
| `commit_msg_template` | string | ‚Äî | Custom commit message template. |
| `conflicts` | list of string | ‚Äî | Packages this PKGBUILD conflicts with. |
| `contributors` | list of string | ‚Äî | Contributors listed in PKGBUILD comments. |
| `depends` | list of string | ‚Äî | Runtime dependencies. |
| `description` | string | ‚Äî | Short description of the package. |
| `directory` | string | ‚Äî | Subdirectory in the git repo for committed files. |
| `git_ssh_command` | string | ‚Äî | Custom SSH command for git operations. |
| `git_url` | string | ‚Äî | AUR SSH git URL override. Defaults to `ssh://aur@aur.archlinux.org/<package>.git`, derived from the resolved package name; set this only for a non-standard endpoint. |
| `homepage` | string | ‚Äî | Project homepage URL. |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build IDs filter. |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the AUR source config is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. The `aur_sources[].if:`. |
| `install` | string | ‚Äî | Content for a .install file (post-install/pre-remove scripts). |
| `license` | string | ‚Äî | SPDX license identifier. |
| `maintainers` | list of string | ‚Äî | PKGBUILD maintainer entries. |
| `makedepends` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build-time dependencies (source packages need these). |
| `name` | string | ‚Äî | Override the package name (default: crate name, no -bin suffix). |
| `optdepends` | list of string | ‚Äî | Optional dependencies. |
| `package` | string | ‚Äî | Custom `package()` function body for PKGBUILD. |
| `prepare` | string | ‚Äî | Custom `prepare()` function body for PKGBUILD. |
| `private_key` | string | ‚Äî | Path to SSH private key file. |
| `provides` | list of string | ‚Äî | Packages this PKGBUILD provides. |
| `rel` | string | ‚Äî | Package release number (default: "1"). |
| `required` | bool | ‚Äî | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.

Default: `false` ‚Äî a failure here is logged but does not abort the release. Set to `true` to fail the release on any error. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip this config. Accepts the legacy `disable:` spelling via serde alias for back-compat. |
| `skip_upload` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip publishing. `"true"` always skips; `"auto"` skips for prereleases. |
| `url_template` | string | ‚Äî | Custom URL template for download URLs. |

## `before`
Top-level lifecycle hooks for `before` and `after` blocks. Each block carries a list of hook commands that run around the entire pipeline (not individual stages).

The canonical key is `hooks:` for both `before:` and `after:` to the conventional spelling. The `post:` spelling is accepted as a serde alias on `hooks` for back-compat with the previous anodizer spelling; users with `after: { post: [...] }` keep working and a deprecation warning is logged when both spellings appear in the same block (see [`HooksConfig::merge_hook_aliases`]).
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `hooks` | list of HookEntry | ‚Äî | Commands to run when the block fires. The wire format accepts either `hooks:` (canonical) or the legacy `post:` spelling; both fold into this field at parse time. |
| `post` | list of HookEntry | ‚Äî | Legacy alias for `hooks:` (anodizer pre-v0.4). Always `None` after parsing ‚Äî `merge_hook_aliases` collapses it into `hooks`. Present on the struct only because `Deserialize` writes through it before the fold step. |

## `before_publish`
Top-level lifecycle hooks for `before` and `after` blocks. Each block carries a list of hook commands that run around the entire pipeline (not individual stages).

The canonical key is `hooks:` for both `before:` and `after:` to the conventional spelling. The `post:` spelling is accepted as a serde alias on `hooks` for back-compat with the previous anodizer spelling; users with `after: { post: [...] }` keep working and a deprecation warning is logged when both spellings appear in the same block (see [`HooksConfig::merge_hook_aliases`]).
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `hooks` | list of HookEntry | ‚Äî | Commands to run when the block fires. The wire format accepts either `hooks:` (canonical) or the legacy `post:` spelling; both fold into this field at parse time. |
| `post` | list of HookEntry | ‚Äî | Legacy alias for `hooks:` (anodizer pre-v0.4). Always `None` after parsing ‚Äî `merge_hook_aliases` collapses it into `hooks`. Present on the struct only because `Deserialize` writes through it before the fold step. |

## `binary_signs`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `args` | list of string | ‚Äî | Arguments passed to the signing command (supports templates with ${artifact} and ${signature}). |
| `artifacts` | string | ‚Äî | Artifact types to sign: "all", "archive", "binary", "checksum", "package", "sbom" (default: "none"). |
| `certificate` | string | ‚Äî | Certificate file to embed in the signature (Cosign bundle signing). |
| `cmd` | string | ‚Äî | Signing command to invoke (default: "cosign" or "gpg"). |
| `env` | list of string | ‚Äî | Environment variables passed to the signing command. |
| `id` | string | ‚Äî | Unique identifier for this sign config. |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build IDs filter: only sign artifacts from builds whose `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional: skip this sign config if rendered result is "false" or empty. |
| `output` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Capture and log stdout/stderr of the signing command. Accepts bool or template string (e.g., "{{ IsSnapshot }}"). |
| `signature` | string | ‚Äî | Signature output filename template (supports templates). |
| `stdin` | string | ‚Äî | Content written to the signing command's stdin. |
| `stdin_file` | string | ‚Äî | Path to a file whose content is written to the signing command's stdin. |

## `changelog`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `abbrev` | integer | ‚Äî | Hash abbreviation length. Default: 0 (no truncation, emit the full SHA). Set to -1 to omit the hash entirely; positive values truncate to N chars. Values below `-1` are clamped to `-1` (a `git log --abbrev=N` would otherwise reject `-2`, `-3`, ...). |
| `ai` | ChangelogAiConfig | ‚Äî | AI-powered changelog enhancement configuration. |
| `divider` | string | ‚Äî | Divider string inserted between changelog groups (e.g. `"---"`). Supports templates. |
| `files` | ChangelogFilesConfig | ‚Äî | Changelog file-layout controls: which `CHANGELOG.md` files a release writes (per-crate vs the aggregate root). Separate from the content-generation keys above (`use`, `format`, `groups`, `filters`, `paths`, `sort`, ...) so file management and content concerns stay orthogonal. See [`ChangelogFilesConfig`]. |
| `filters` | ChangelogFilters | ‚Äî | Commit message filters to include or exclude from the changelog. |
| `footer` | ContentSource | ‚Äî | Text appended to the changelog. Same shape as `header`. |
| `format` | string | ‚Äî | Template for each changelog commit line. Available variables: SHA (full hash), ShortSHA (abbreviated), Message (commit subject), AuthorName, AuthorEmail, Login (per-commit GitHub username, `github` backend only), Logins (per-entry comma-separated list of GitHub usernames for that commit, `github` backend only), AllLogins (comma-separated list of all GitHub usernames across the entire release, `github` backend only).<br><br>Default depends on backend (the full SHA is used):<br>&bull; `git` backend (default): `"{{ SHA }} {{ Message }}"`<br>&bull; `github`/`gitlab`/`gitea` backend: `"{{ SHA }}: {{ Message }} (@Login or AuthorName <AuthorEmail>)"` ‚Äî falls back to `AuthorName <AuthorEmail>` when `Login` is empty.<br><br>When `abbrev < 0`, the default reduces to `"{{ Message }}"` (no hash prefix). |
| `groups` | list of ChangelogGroup | ‚Äî | Groups for organizing changelog entries by commit message prefix. |
| `header` | ContentSource | ‚Äî | Text prepended to the changelog. Inline string, `from_file: <path>`, or `from_url: <url>` ‚Äî symmetric with the release block's header/footer so users can compose headers from a templated file or remote endpoint (the upstream uses a plain string here; anodizer extends to ContentSource for consistency with `release.header`). |
| `paths` | list of string | ‚Äî | Optional path filter that NARROWS the per-crate scope by intersection ‚Äî it never replaces it. Each changelog track already scopes to its own commits (a per-crate track to its crate directory; the aggregate to the union of every crate directory plus the workspace manifests). When set, `paths` further restricts that derived scope to commits whose touched files match these globs; it can only ever drop commits, never widen to another track's directory. A `paths` value that is a superset of the derived scope (e.g. `["crates/**", "Cargo.toml", "Cargo.lock"]` over a workspace) is therefore a no-op ‚Äî and so is the recommended default of leaving `paths` unset, where scoping is fully derived. The same derived scope and intersect drive all three changelog formats (`keep-a-changelog`, `json`, and `release-notes`), so they cannot drift.

With `use: git` the intersect is precise (commits are filtered by their touched files). With `use: github` only the first path is used for API queries; with `use: gitlab` / `gitea` path filtering is unsupported, so a narrowing `paths` there is coarse and a warning is emitted. Supports template rendering. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip changelog generation. Accepts bool or template string (e.g. `"{{ if IsSnapshot }}true{{ endif }}"` for conditional skip).

Accepts `disable:` as an alias so imported configs (which use `changelog.disable:`) parse cleanly without a rename. Anodizer's broader convention is `skip:` (mirrors `release.skip_upload`, stage-level `skip:` flags), so the canonical key stays `skip:`. |
| `snapshot` | bool | ‚Äî | When `true`, render the changelog even in snapshot mode. Anodizer matches the default (skip changelog on snapshot) and lets users opt back in here for local preview / draft generation. Wired in `crates/stage-changelog/src/lib.rs::ChangelogStage::run`. |
| `sort` | string | ‚Äî | Sort order for changelog entries: "asc" or "desc" (default: "asc"). |
| `title` | string | ‚Äî | Title heading for the changelog. Default: "Changelog". Supports templates. |
| `use` | string | ‚Äî | Changelog source: `"git"` (default), `"github"`, or `"github-native"`. `"github"` fetches commits via the GitHub API, enriching entries with author login information (available as the `{{ Logins }}` per-entry template variable and the `{{ AllLogins }}` release-wide variable). `"github-native"` delegates entirely to GitHub's auto-generated notes. |

## `cloudsmiths`
CloudSmith publisher configuration. Pushes packages to CloudSmith repositories.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `component` | string | ‚Äî | Debian component name (e.g. "main"). |
| `distributions` | map | ‚Äî | Distribution mapping per format. Each entry accepts either a single slug (`deb: "ubuntu/focal"`) or an array of slugs (`deb: ["ubuntu/focal", "ubuntu/jammy"]`); the array form issues one upload per entry. |
| `formats` | list of string | ‚Äî | Package format filter: only publish artifacts matching these formats. |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build IDs filter: only publish artifacts from builds whose `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the CloudSmith publisher is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. Config key: `cloudsmiths[].if:`. |
| `organization` | string | ‚Äî | CloudSmith organization slug. |
| `repository` | string | ‚Äî | CloudSmith repository slug. |
| `republish` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | When true, allow republishing over existing package versions. |
| `required` | bool | ‚Äî | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.

Default: `false` ‚Äî a failure here is logged but does not abort the release. Set to `true` to fail the release on any error. |
| `secret_name` | string | ‚Äî | Environment variable name containing the CloudSmith API key. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Template-conditional skip: if rendered result is `"true"`, skip this publisher. |

## `crates`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `app_bundles` | list of AppBundleConfig | ‚Äî | macOS app bundle configurations for this crate. |
| `archives` | list of ArchiveConfig | `[]` | Archive configurations for this crate. Set to false to disable archiving, or provide an array of archive configs. |
| `binstall` | BinstallConfig | ‚Äî | cargo-binstall metadata configuration for this crate. |
| `blobs` | list of BlobConfig | ‚Äî | Cloud storage (S3/GCS/Azure) upload configurations for this crate. |
| `builds` | list of BuildConfig | ‚Äî | Build configurations for this crate. One entry per binary by default. |
| `checksum` | ChecksumConfig | ‚Äî | Checksum configuration for this crate. |
| `cross` | CrossStrategy | ‚Äî | Cross-compilation strategy for this crate: auto, zigbuild, cross, or cargo. |
| `depends_on` | list of string | ‚Äî | Other crates this crate depends on; ensures release ordering. |
| `dmgs` | list of DmgConfig | ‚Äî | macOS DMG disk image configurations for this crate. |
| `docker_digest` | DockerDigestConfig | ‚Äî | Docker image digest file configuration for this crate. |
| `docker_manifests` | list of DockerManifestConfig | ‚Äî | Docker multi-platform manifest configurations for this crate. |
| `dockers_v2` | list of DockerV2Config | ‚Äî | Docker V2 image build configurations for this crate (canonical API: images+tags, annotations, build_args, sbom, disable). The legacy `docker:` block was removed; this is the only docker surface. The `docker_v2:` spelling is still accepted via serde alias for back-compat. |
| `flatpaks` | list of FlatpakConfig | ‚Äî | Linux Flatpak bundle configurations for this crate. |
| `msis` | list of MsiConfig | ‚Äî | Windows MSI installer configurations for this crate. |
| `name` | string | ‚Äî | Crate name as published (must match the Cargo.toml package name). |
| `nfpms` | list of NfpmConfig | ‚Äî | Linux package (deb, rpm, apk) configurations for this crate. Renamed from `nfpm:` (singular) for spelling parity with `Defaults.nfpms` and the rest of the plural-name per-crate packaging lists (`dmgs`, `msis`, `pkgs`, `nsis`, ...). The `nfpm:` spelling is still accepted via serde alias for back-compat. |
| `no_unique_dist_dir` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | When true (or template evaluating to "true"), all build outputs are placed in a flat `dist/` directory instead of `dist/{target}/`. |
| `nsis` | list of NsisConfig | ‚Äî | NSIS installer configurations for this crate. |
| `path` | string | ‚Äî | Relative path to the crate directory from the project root. |
| `pkgs` | list of PkgConfig | ‚Äî | macOS PKG installer configurations for this crate. |
| `publish` | PublishConfig | ‚Äî | Publishing targets (Homebrew, Scoop, AUR, etc.) for this crate. |
| `release` | ReleaseConfig | ‚Äî | GitHub release configuration for this crate. |
| `snapcrafts` | list of SnapcraftConfig | ‚Äî | Snapcraft package configurations for this crate. |
| `tag_template` | string | ‚Äî | Git tag template used to tag and identify releases (supports templates). |
| `universal_binaries` | list of UniversalBinaryConfig | ‚Äî | macOS universal binary (fat binary) configurations for this crate. |
| `version` | string | ‚Äî | Pinned semver version. When set, `anodizer bump --strict` refuses to edit this crate's `Cargo.toml` to anything other than this value; without `--strict`, the bump proceeds with a warning. Lets a release captain freeze a crate's version while still running broad `--workspace` bumps. |
| `version_files` | list of string | ‚Äî | Repo-committed files that embed this crate's release version outside `Cargo.toml` (repo-root-relative path strings). At `tag` time each file has its occurrences of the old version rewritten to the new version ‚Äî both bare and `v`-prefixed forms, word-boundary anchored ‚Äî and is staged into the same bump commit as this crate's `Cargo.toml`. Overrides the workspace-level `defaults.version_files`. |
| `version_sync` | VersionSyncConfig | ‚Äî | Automatic version number synchronization configuration for this crate. |

## `defaults`
Workspace-level defaults that path-mirror the `CrateConfig` (and select top-level `Config`) shape. Each field here is folded into every resolved crate by `defaults_merge::apply_defaults` according to the deep-merge / merge-by-identity semantics documented in `defaults_merge`.

Multi-publisher fields are single-struct on both sides today: defaults supplies one struct per publisher, and per-crate `publish.*` fields are also single-struct. A future change may introduce list-or-scalar via `OneOrMany<T>` on the per-crate side so a crate can declare multiple homebrew taps / scoop buckets / etc.; the defaults side would stay single-struct and merge into the first per-crate entry by identity.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `app_bundles` | AppBundleConfig | ‚Äî | Default app-bundle settings applied to all crates. |
| `archives` | ArchiveConfig | ‚Äî | Default archive settings applied to all crates. |
| `binary_signs` | SignConfig | ‚Äî | Default binary-signing settings. |
| `builds` | BuildConfig | ‚Äî | Default build settings applied to every crate's builds (deep-merged into each `CrateConfig.builds[]` entry by identity on `id`/`binary`). |
| `checksum` | ChecksumConfig | ‚Äî | Default checksum settings applied to all crates. Mirrors `CrateConfig.checksum` so checksum config can be hoisted to defaults. |
| `crates` | DefaultsCrateBlock | ‚Äî | Crate-axis defaults marker. Only valid when top-level `crates:` is set. Reserved for per-crate overrides keyed by crate id (future waves). |
| `cross` | CrossStrategy | ‚Äî | Default cross-compilation strategy: auto, zigbuild, cross, or cargo. Mirrors `CrateConfig.cross` so the strategy can be hoisted to defaults. |
| `dmgs` | DmgConfig | ‚Äî | Default DMG settings applied to all crates. |
| `docker_signs` | DockerSignConfig | ‚Äî | Default Docker image signing settings. |
| `dockers_v2` | DockerV2Config | ‚Äî | Default Docker (V2 API) image settings applied to all crates. The `docker_v2:` spelling is still accepted via serde alias for back-compat. |
| `env` | list of string | ‚Äî | Default environment variables (`KEY=VALUE` strings) hoisted across crates. |
| `flatpaks` | FlatpakConfig | ‚Äî | Default flatpak settings applied to all crates. |
| `makeselves` | MakeselfConfig | ‚Äî | Default makeself settings applied to all crates. |
| `msis` | MsiConfig | ‚Äî | Default MSI settings applied to all crates. |
| `nfpms` | NfpmConfig | ‚Äî | Default nfpm (deb/rpm/apk) settings applied to all crates. |
| `notarize` | NotarizeConfig | ‚Äî | Default macOS notarization settings. |
| `nsis` | NsisConfig | ‚Äî | Default NSIS settings applied to all crates. |
| `pkgs` | PkgConfig | ‚Äî | Default macOS PKG settings applied to all crates. |
| `publish` | PublishDefaults | ‚Äî | Default publisher configurations (single-struct per publisher). Per-crate `publish.*` entries are merged into these by identity. |
| `sbom` | SbomConfig | ‚Äî | Default SBOM generation settings. |
| `sign` | SignConfig | ‚Äî | Default artifact signing settings. |
| `snapcrafts` | SnapcraftConfig | ‚Äî | Default snapcraft settings applied to all crates. |
| `source` | SourceConfig | ‚Äî | Default source-archive settings applied to all crates. |
| `srpms` | SrpmConfig | ‚Äî | Default SRPM settings applied to all crates. |
| `targets` | list of string | ‚Äî | Default build targets (e.g., ["x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu", "aarch64-apple-darwin"]). |
| `upx` | UpxConfig | ‚Äî | Default UPX compression settings applied to all crates. |
| `version_files` | list of string | ‚Äî | Default repo-committed files whose embedded release version is rewritten at `tag` time (repo-root-relative path strings). Hoisted across crates; folded into each crate's `version_files` by `defaults_merge` when the crate does not set its own list. Mirrors `CrateConfig.version_files`. |
| `workspaces` | DefaultsWorkspaceBlock | ‚Äî | Workspace-axis defaults marker. Only valid when top-level `workspaces:` is set. Reserved for per-workspace overrides keyed by workspace name (future waves). |

## `docker_signs`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `args` | list of string | ‚Äî | Arguments passed to the signing command (supports templates). |
| `artifacts` | string | ‚Äî | Docker artifact types to sign: "all", "image", or "manifest" (default: "none"). |
| `certificate` | string | ‚Äî | Certificate file to embed in the signature (Cosign bundle signing). |
| `cmd` | string | ‚Äî | Signing command to invoke (default: "cosign"). |
| `env` | list of string | ‚Äî | Environment variables passed to the signing command. |
| `id` | string | ‚Äî | Unique identifier for this docker sign config. |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Docker config IDs filter: only sign images from configs whose `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional: skip this docker sign config if rendered result is "false" or empty. |
| `output` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Capture and log stdout/stderr of the docker signing command. |
| `signature` | string | ‚Äî | Signature output filename template (supports templates). |
| `stdin` | string | ‚Äî | Content written to the signing command's stdin. |
| `stdin_file` | string | ‚Äî | Path to a file whose content is written to the signing command's stdin. |

## `dockerhub`
DockerHub description sync configuration. Pushes image descriptions and README content to DockerHub repositories.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `description` | string | ‚Äî | Short description for the DockerHub repository (max 100 chars). |
| `full_description` | DockerHubFullDescription | ‚Äî | Full description (README) source for the DockerHub repository. |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the DockerHub publisher is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. The `dockerhub[].if:`. |
| `images` | list of string | ‚Äî | DockerHub image names to update (e.g. `myorg/myapp`). |
| `required` | bool | ‚Äî | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.

Default: `false` ‚Äî a failure here is logged but does not abort the release. Set to `true` to fail the release on any error. |
| `secret_name` | string | ‚Äî | Environment variable name containing the DockerHub token. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip this publisher. Accepts bool or template string. Accepts the legacy `disable:` spelling via serde alias for back-compat with imported configs (the legacy `disable:` spelling). |
| `username` | string | ‚Äî | DockerHub username for authentication. |

## `gemfury`
GemFury package registry publisher configuration.

Pushes deb / rpm / apk artifacts to `https://push.fury.io/<account>`. Authenticates via HTTP Basic auth using the push token as the username (empty password) ‚Äî the conventional Fury push surface.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `account` | string | ‚Äî | GemFury account name. Required; rendered through the template engine so `account: "{{ Env.MY_FURY_ACCOUNT }}"` works. |
| `api_secret_name` | string | ‚Äî | Environment variable name carrying the API (delete) token. Default `FURY_API_TOKEN`. |
| `api_token` | string | ‚Äî | Optional API token used by rollback to issue `DELETE /<account>/packages/<name>/versions/<version>`. When unset, the env var named by `api_secret_name` (default `FURY_API_TOKEN`) is consulted at rollback time. If both are absent at rollback time, the publisher falls back to a manual-cleanup warn. |
| `formats` | list of string | ‚Äî | Package format filter: only push artifacts matching these formats. Defaults to `["apk", "deb", "rpm"]`. |
| `id` | string | ‚Äî | Unique identifier for selecting this entry from the CLI (`--id=...`). |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build IDs filter: only include artifacts whose archive `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the GemFury publisher entry is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. The `gemfury[].if:`. |
| `required` | bool | ‚Äî | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.

Default: `true` ‚Äî GemFury is a Manager-group publisher (mutable but reversible via the delete API), so a failed publish aborts by default to avoid surprising the operator with a half-released version. Set to `false` to log failures but continue. |
| `secret_name` | string | ‚Äî | Environment variable name carrying the push token. Default `FURY_TOKEN`. The actual token VALUE is read from this env var at publish/rollback time. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Template-conditional skip: if rendered result is `"true"`, skip this publisher entry. Accepts bool or template string. Accepts the legacy `disable:` spelling via serde alias for back-compat with imported `gemfury[].disable:` configs. |
| `token` | string | ‚Äî | Push token used as the HTTP Basic auth username (empty password). When unset, the env var named by `secret_name` (default `FURY_TOKEN`) is consulted at publish time. NEVER logged. |

## `git`
Git-level tag discovery and sorting settings.

Controls how anodizer discovers and orders tags when determining the current and previous versions. This is separate from `TagConfig`, which controls version *bumping* logic.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `ignore_tag_prefixes` | list of string | ‚Äî | Tag prefixes to ignore during version detection (supports templates). Tags starting with any prefix in this list are excluded. The ignore-tag-prefixes feature. |
| `ignore_tags` | list of string | ‚Äî | Tag patterns to ignore during version detection (supports templates). Tags matching any pattern in this list are excluded from version detection entirely. |
| `prerelease_suffix` | string | ‚Äî | Suffix that identifies pre-release tags for sorting purposes. When set, tags ending with this suffix are treated as pre-releases and sorted accordingly during tag discovery. |
| `tag_sort` | string | ‚Äî | How to sort git tags when determining the latest version.

Accepted values: - `"-version:refname"` (default) ‚Äî lexicographic version sort on the tag name. - `"-version:creatordate"` ‚Äî sort by the tag's creation date (newest first). - `"semver"` ‚Äî strict SemVer 2.0.0 ordering computed in Rust; prereleases sort below their release per spec section 11. Bypasses git's native sort. - `"smartsemver"` ‚Äî same ordering as `"semver"`, but when the current version (resolved from the template `Version` variable) is non-prerelease, prerelease tags are filtered out before previous-tag selection. Prevents `v0.2.0-beta.3` from being picked as the predecessor of `v0.2.0` (which would otherwise produce an empty changelog). |

## `gitea_urls`
Custom Gitea API/download URLs for self-hosted Gitea installations. Gitea API/download URL overrides.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `api` | string | ‚Äî | Gitea API base URL (e.g. `https://gitea.example.com/api/v1/`). |
| `download` | string | ‚Äî | Gitea download URL for release assets. |
| `skip_tls_verify` | bool | ‚Äî | When true, skip TLS certificate verification for the custom URLs. |

## `github_urls`
Custom GitHub API/upload/download URLs for GitHub Enterprise installations. GitHub API/download URL overrides.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `api` | string | ‚Äî | GitHub API base URL (e.g. `https://github.example.com/api/v3/`). |
| `download` | string | ‚Äî | GitHub download URL for release assets (e.g. `https://github.example.com/`). |
| `skip_tls_verify` | bool | ‚Äî | When true, skip TLS certificate verification for the custom URLs. |
| `upload` | string | ‚Äî | GitHub upload URL for release assets (e.g. `https://github.example.com/api/uploads/`). |

## `gitlab_urls`
Custom GitLab API/download URLs for self-hosted GitLab installations. GitLab API/download URL overrides.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `api` | string | ‚Äî | GitLab API base URL (e.g. `https://gitlab.example.com/api/v4/`). |
| `download` | string | ‚Äî | GitLab download URL for release assets. |
| `skip_tls_verify` | bool | ‚Äî | When true, skip TLS certificate verification for the custom URLs. |
| `use_job_token` | bool | ‚Äî | When true, use the CI_JOB_TOKEN for authentication instead of a personal token. |
| `use_package_registry` | bool | ‚Äî | When true, use the GitLab Package Registry for uploads instead of Generic Packages. |

## `homebrew_casks`
Unified Homebrew Cask configuration.

Used at both call-sites: - `homebrew_casks:` ‚Äî top-level array; carries `repository`, `commit_author`, `directory`, `ids`, `url`, structured `uninstall`/`zap`, etc. - `crates[].publish.homebrew_cask:` ‚Äî per-crate override; same shape, with `url_template` as the simpler URL alternative.

Fields from both original types are present; any field may be `None` at either call-site. The union avoids a two-type bifurcation while keeping both axes.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `alternative_names` | list of string | ‚Äî | Alternative cask names (aliases). |
| `app` | string | ‚Äî | macOS .app bundle name (e.g. "MyApp.app"). |
| `binaries` | list of HomebrewCaskBinary | ‚Äî | Binary stubs to create in /usr/local/bin.

Each entry is either a bare string (`"my-cli"` ‚Üí emits `binary "my-cli"`) or a structured `{ name, target }` object (`{ name: "my-cli", target: "mycli" }` ‚Üí emits `binary "my-cli", target: "mycli"`). The `target:` form mirrors the Homebrew Ruby cask DSL for binary renames ‚Äî without it, a wrapped binary installs at the wrong path. Cask binary entry. |
| `binary` | string | ‚Äî | Deprecated singular spelling of [`Self::binaries`]. The upstream replaced `binary: foo` with `binaries: [foo]`; this field captures the legacy spelling so imported configs keep parsing. [`apply_homebrew_cask_legacy_singulars`](super::super::apply_homebrew_cask_legacy_singulars) folds the value into [`Self::binaries`] at config-load time and emits a one-time deprecation warning per occurrence. The field is excluded from serialization so a round-tripped config emits only the canonical plural form. |
| `caveats` | string | ‚Äî | Custom caveats shown after install. |
| `commit_author` | CommitAuthorConfig | ‚Äî | Commit author with optional signing. |
| `commit_msg_template` | string | ‚Äî | Custom commit message template. Default: "Brew cask update for {{ ProjectName }} version {{ Tag }}" |
| `completions` | HomebrewCaskCompletions | ‚Äî | Shell completion definitions. |
| `conflicts` | list of HomebrewCaskConflictEntry | ‚Äî | Conflicting casks or formulae. |
| `custom_block` | string | ‚Äî | Arbitrary Ruby code inserted into the cask block. |
| `dependencies` | list of HomebrewCaskDependencyEntry | ‚Äî | Cask dependencies (other casks or formulae). |
| `description` | string | ‚Äî | Cask description. |
| `directory` | string | ‚Äî | Subdirectory in the tap repo for cask placement (default: "Casks"). |
| `generate_completions_from_executable` | HomebrewCaskGeneratedCompletions | ‚Äî | Auto-generate shell completions from an executable. |
| `homepage` | string | ‚Äî | Project homepage URL. |
| `hooks` | HomebrewCaskHooks | ‚Äî | Pre/post install/uninstall hooks. |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build IDs filter: only include artifacts from builds whose `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the Homebrew Cask config is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. Config key: `homebrew_casks[].if:`. |
| `license` | string | ‚Äî | License identifier (SPDX). |
| `manpage` | string | ‚Äî | Deprecated singular spelling of [`Self::manpages`]. The upstream replaced `manpage: foo.1` with `manpages: [foo.1]`; this field captures the legacy spelling so imported configs keep parsing. [`apply_homebrew_cask_legacy_singulars`](super::super::apply_homebrew_cask_legacy_singulars) folds the value into [`Self::manpages`] at config-load time and emits a one-time deprecation warning per occurrence. The field is excluded from serialization so a round-tripped config emits only the canonical plural form. |
| `manpages` | list of string | ‚Äî | Manual page references to install. |
| `name` | string | ‚Äî | Cask name (default: crate / project name). |
| `repository` | RepositoryConfig | ‚Äî | Unified repository config for the Homebrew tap. |
| `required` | bool | ‚Äî | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.

Default: `false` ‚Äî a failure here is logged but does not abort the release. Set to `true` to fail the release on any error. |
| `service` | string | ‚Äî | Homebrew service definition. |
| `skip_upload` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip publishing the cask. `"true"` always skips; `"auto"` skips for prerelease versions. Accepts bool or template string. |
| `uninstall` | HomebrewCaskUninstall | ‚Äî | Structured uninstall stanza configuration. |
| `update_existing_pr` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | When true, force-push the updated cask file to the existing PR branch when a PR for the same head branch already exists. The PR content is updated in place rather than creating a duplicate. When false (default), the push is skipped and a warning is emitted so the operator sees that the publisher did not update the PR. |
| `url` | HomebrewCaskURL | ‚Äî | Structured download URL configuration (top-level axis). |
| `url_template` | string | ‚Äî | Simple URL template for the .dmg/.zip download (per-crate shorthand).

Cannot be combined with `url.template:` ‚Äî set one or the other. If both are present, config validation rejects the config at parse time. Use `url:` for the structured form (verified domain, custom headers, etc.) or `url_template:` for a bare string shorthand ‚Äî never both simultaneously. |
| `zap` | HomebrewCaskUninstall | ‚Äî | Deep uninstall (zap) stanza configuration. |

## `makeselfs`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `arch` | list of string | ‚Äî | Target architecture filter. |
| `compression` | string | ‚Äî | Compression algorithm: gzip, bzip2, xz, lzo, compress, or none. |
| `description` | string | ‚Äî | Description for LSM metadata. |
| `extra_args` | list of string | ‚Äî | Extra arguments passed to the makeself command. |
| `filename` | string | ‚Äî | Output filename template (default includes project, version, os, arch). |
| `files` | list of MakeselfFile | ‚Äî | Additional files to include in the archive. |
| `homepage` | string | ‚Äî | Homepage URL for LSM metadata. |
| `id` | string | ‚Äî | Unique identifier for this makeself config (default: "default"). |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build IDs filter: only include artifacts whose `id` is in this list. |
| `keywords` | list of string | ‚Äî | Keywords for LSM metadata. |
| `license` | string | ‚Äî | License for LSM metadata. |
| `maintainer` | string | ‚Äî | Maintainer for LSM metadata. |
| `name` | string | ‚Äî | Display name embedded in the self-extracting archive. |
| `os` | list of string | ‚Äî | Target OS filter (default: ["linux", "darwin"]). |
| `script` | string | ‚Äî | Startup script to run when the archive is extracted and executed. Required ‚Äî the archive will not be created without this. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip this config. Accepts bool or template string. Accepts the legacy `disable:` spelling via serde alias for back-compat with imported configs. |

## `mcp`
MCP server registry publisher configuration.

Publishes an `apiv0.ServerJSON` document to the MCP registry (`https://registry.modelcontextprotocol.io/v0/publish` by default). MCP config (server details flattened onto the publisher block).
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `auth` | McpAuth | `{"type":"none"}` | Authentication method for the registry's `/v0/publish` endpoint. Defaults to `none` (anonymous publish, allowed for development / staging registries). |
| `description` | string | ‚Äî | Clear human-readable description of server functionality (max 100 chars). |
| `homepage` | string | ‚Äî | Optional URL to the server's homepage, documentation, or project website. Serialized as `websiteUrl` in the registry payload. |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the MCP publisher is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. The `mcp.if:` conditional gate. |
| `name` | string | ‚Äî | Server name in reverse-DNS format (e.g. `io.github.user/weather`). Must contain exactly one forward slash separating namespace from server name. An empty / unset value skips the publisher entirely. |
| `packages` | list of McpPackage | `[]` | Distribution packages ‚Äî one entry per package registry (npm, pypi, nuget, oci, mcpb). |
| `registry` | string | ‚Äî | Override the registry endpoint (for staging or a private mirror). Defaults to `https://registry.modelcontextprotocol.io` when unset. |
| `repository` | McpRepository | `{"url":"","source":"","id":"","subfolder":""}` | Optional source repository metadata. Emitted as the `repository` object in the registry payload ‚Äî omitted entirely when `url` is empty. |
| `required` | bool | ‚Äî | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.

Default: `false` ‚Äî a failure here is logged but does not abort the release. Set to `true` to fail the release on any error. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip this publisher when the expression evaluates truthy. Accepts a bool or a Tera template that renders to `"true"`/`"false"` (e.g. `"{{ if .IsSnapshot }}true{{ endif }}"`). Accepts the legacy `disable:` spelling via serde alias for back-compat with imported imported configs (the MCP config field `MCP.Disable string`). |
| `title` | string | ‚Äî | Optional human-readable title shown in registry UIs (max 100 chars). Templated; supports `{{ ProjectName \| title }}`, `{{ Version }}`, etc. |
| `transports` | list of McpTransport | `[]` | Top-level transports list. Intentional config-portability shim: `McpConfig` carries `deny_unknown_fields`, so a migrated an imported config containing `transports:` would fail to parse if the field were absent. The list is accepted and discarded ‚Äî the current MCP server schema derives transports per-package via `packages[].transport`, so the top-level list is never read after deserialization and is intentionally not emitted to the registry. |

## `metadata`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `commit_author` | CommitAuthorConfig | ‚Äî | Commit author identity for commit workflows. Reuses the shared `CommitAuthorConfig` (name + email + optional signing). Exposed as `{{ Metadata.CommitAuthor.Name }}` / `{{ Metadata.CommitAuthor.Email }}`. |
| `description` | string | ‚Äî | Human-readable project description (exposed as `{{ Metadata.Description }}`). |
| `full_description` | ContentSource | ‚Äî | Long-form project description. Supports inline string, `from_file`, or `from_url`. Exposed as `{{ Metadata.FullDescription }}`. FromUrl is resolved lazily (requires the release stage); FromFile is resolved at context-populate time with template-rendered path. |
| `homepage` | string | ‚Äî | Project homepage URL (exposed as `{{ Metadata.Homepage }}`). |
| `license` | string | ‚Äî | Project license identifier, e.g. "MIT" or "Apache-2.0" (exposed as `{{ Metadata.License }}`). |
| `maintainers` | list of string | ‚Äî | List of project maintainers (exposed as `{{ Metadata.Maintainers }}`). |
| `mod_timestamp` | string | ‚Äî | Global modification timestamp for metadata output files (metadata.json and artifacts.json). Template string (e.g. "{{ CommitTimestamp }}") or unix timestamp. When set, rendered late in the pipeline and applied as file mtime. Exposed as `{{ Metadata.ModTimestamp }}`. |

## `milestones`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `close` | bool | ‚Äî | Close the milestone on release. Default: false. |
| `fail_on_error` | bool | ‚Äî | Fail the pipeline if milestone close fails. Default: false. |
| `name_template` | string | ‚Äî | Milestone name template (default: "{{ Tag }}"). |
| `repo` | ScmRepoConfig | ‚Äî | Repository owner/name. Auto-detected from git remote if not set. |

## `monorepo`
Monorepo configuration.

When configured, tag discovery filters by `tag_prefix` and the working directory is scoped to `dir`.

This is DIFFERENT from `TagConfig.tag_prefix`: - `MonorepoConfig.tag_prefix`: tags in git already HAVE the prefix (e.g. `subproject1/v1.2.3`). The prefix is STRIPPED for `{{ Tag }}` while `{{ PrefixedTag }}` retains the full tag. - `TagConfig.tag_prefix`: a prefix to PREPEND when constructing `{{ PrefixedTag }}` from a plain tag.

When `monorepo` is configured, it takes precedence over `tag.tag_prefix` for `PrefixedTag` / `PrefixedPreviousTag` behavior.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `dir` | string | ‚Äî | Working directory for this subproject.

Used for changelog path filtering (when no explicit `changelog.paths` or `crate.path` is configured) and as the default build `dir`. |
| `tag_prefix` | string | ‚Äî | Tag prefix for this subproject (e.g. `"subproject1/"`).

Tags matching this prefix are selected during tag discovery, and the prefix is stripped from `{{ Tag }}` while `{{ PrefixedTag }}` retains the full tag. |

## `nightly`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `draft` | bool | ‚Äî | Override `release.draft` for nightly runs only. `None` falls through to `release.draft`; `Some(v)` overrides it. |
| `keep_single_release` | bool | ‚Äî | Delete the prior release that points at the same tag before creating the new one. Default: `false`. Set `true` to maintain a single rolling nightly release on GitHub.

Back-compat alias for `retention: { keep_last: 1 }`. When both `keep_single_release` and `retention` are set, `retention` wins. Destructive: deletes a published release via the GitHub Releases API. GitHub-only. |
| `name_template` | string | ‚Äî | Template for the release name. Default: `"{{ ProjectName }}-nightly"`. |
| `publish_release` | bool | ‚Äî | Whether to publish a GitHub Release at all. Default: `true`. Set `false` for nightly-only docker pushes / blob uploads. |
| `publish_repo` | string | ‚Äî | Publish the nightly release to a DIFFERENT repository than the source repo, in `"owner/repo"` form (e.g. `"nushell/nightly"`). Default (`None`) publishes to the configured `release.github` repo, unchanged.

When set, the nightly release create, asset upload, AND retention (`keep_single_release` / `retention.keep_last`) delete calls all target this repo. The active SCM token is assumed to have write access to `publish_repo`. GitHub-only (the nushell adoption target). |
| `retention` | RetentionConfig | ‚Äî | Retention policy for nightly releases on GitHub. Generalizes `keep_single_release` (which is `keep_last: 1`): keeps the N newest nightly releases matching the nightly tag/name and deletes the rest (releases + the tags anodizer created for them). Operates on `publish_repo` when set. Default (`None`): no retention sweep. |
| `tag_name` | string | ‚Äî | Tag name used for the nightly release. Default: `"nightly"`. Templates allowed. |
| `version_template` | string | ‚Äî | Template for the rendered version string the nightly run sets on `Version` / `RawVersion`. Default: `"{{ incpatch(v=Version) }}-{{ ShortCommit }}-nightly"` ‚Äî produces commit-immutable nightly versions (two same-day commits yield two distinct nightly versions).

The `{{ NightlyBuild }}` template var (a stateless per-base-version build counter derived from `git rev-list --count <last-tag>..HEAD`) enables nushell-style schemes such as `"{{ Base }}-nightly.{{ NightlyBuild }}+{{ ShortCommit }}"`. |

## `notarize`
Top-level notarization configuration supporting both cross-platform (`rcodesign`) and native macOS (`codesign` + `xcrun notarytool`) modes.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `macos` | list of MacOSSignNotarizeConfig | ‚Äî | Cross-platform signing/notarization (rcodesign-based, works on any OS). |
| `macos_native` | list of MacOSNativeSignNotarizeConfig | ‚Äî | Native signing/notarization (codesign + xcrun, macOS only). |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip all notarization. Accepts bool or template string. |

## `npms`
NPM package registry publisher configuration.

In the default `optional-deps` mode anodizer emits one thin npm package per built platform (with `os`/`cpu`/`libc` selectors derived from the target triple) plus a metapackage whose `optionalDependencies` lists every platform package; npm's native resolution installs only the one matching the host. In `postinstall` mode a single package carries a `postinstall` script that downloads the matching release archive at `npm install` time. Each `npms[]` entry produces one publish.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `access` | string | ‚Äî | NPM access level for scoped packages. Accepts `"public"` / `"restricted"`. Scoped packages on npmjs.org default to `restricted` unless this is set to `public`. |
| `author` | string | ‚Äî | Templated `author` field for `package.json`. Falls back to `metadata.maintainers[0]` when unset. |
| `bin` | string | ‚Äî | Command name installed by the metapackage's `bin` map (`optional-deps` mode). Falls back to the metapackage basename when unset. |
| `bugs` | string | ‚Äî | Templated bug tracker URL. Emitted as `bugs.url` in `package.json`. |
| `description` | string | ‚Äî | Templated package description. Falls back to the project-level `metadata.description` when unset. |
| `extra` | map | ‚Äî | Free-form root-level `package.json` fields. Shallow-merged into the generated `package.json`. Useful for `engines`, `mcpName`, or other npm metadata fields anodizer does not surface. |
| `extra_files` | list of string | ‚Äî | Additional files to include in the published package alongside the generated metadata. Default `["README*", "LICENSE*"]` (applied at the `Default` pass). |
| `format` | string | ‚Äî | Archive format the `postinstall` script downloads (`tgz`, `tar.gz`, `tar`, `zip`, `binary`). Default `tgz`. Only consulted in `postinstall` mode. |
| `homepage` | string | ‚Äî | Templated homepage URL. Falls back to `metadata.homepage` when unset. |
| `id` | string | ‚Äî | Unique identifier for selecting this entry from the CLI (`--id=...`). |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build IDs filter: only include artifacts whose archive `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the NPM publisher entry is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. |
| `keywords` | list of string | ‚Äî | NPM `keywords` list. |
| `libc_aware` | bool | `true` | In `optional-deps` mode, emit separate per-platform packages for linux `musl` vs `glibc` (distinguished by the npm `libc` selector). When `false`, a single linux package per cpu is emitted with no `libc` selector. Default `true` ‚Äî musl and glibc binaries are not interchangeable, so collapsing them risks installing the wrong one. |
| `license` | string | ‚Äî | Templated SPDX license identifier (e.g. `MIT`, `Apache-2.0`). Falls back to `metadata.license` when unset. |
| `metapackage` | string | ‚Äî | Metapackage name for `optional-deps` mode (e.g. `biome`). This is the package users `npm install`; it lists every per-platform package under `optionalDependencies` and ships the `bin` shim. Falls back to `name` (or the crate name) when unset. |
| `mode` | NpmMode | `optional-deps` | Binary-distribution strategy. `optional-deps` (default) emits npm's native per-platform packages; `postinstall` emits a download shim. |
| `name` | string | ‚Äî | NPM package name (the metapackage / postinstall package). May be scoped (`@org/foo`) or unscoped (`foo`). Falls back to the crate name when unset. |
| `registry` | string | ‚Äî | Override the registry endpoint (default `https://registry.npmjs.org`). |
| `repository` | string | ‚Äî | Templated repository URL. Emitted as `repository.url` in `package.json` with `type: git`. |
| `required` | bool | ‚Äî | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.

Default: `true` ‚Äî NPM is a Manager-group publisher (one-way 72-hour unpublish window), so a failed publish aborts by default to avoid surprising the operator with a half-released version. Set to `false` to log failures but continue. |
| `scope` | string | ‚Äî | npm scope for the per-platform packages emitted in `optional-deps` mode (e.g. `@biomejs`). The per-platform packages are named `<scope>/<bin>-<os>-<cpu>[-<libc>]`. Required for `optional-deps` mode; ignored in `postinstall` mode. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip this publisher. Accepts bool or template string. Accepts the legacy `disable:` spelling via serde alias for back-compat. |
| `tag` | string | ‚Äî | NPM dist-tag for the publish (default `latest`). Templated. |
| `templated_extra_files` | list of NpmTemplatedExtraFile | ‚Äî | Template-rendered file mappings (`src` may be a glob; rendered contents written to `dst`). |
| `token` | string | ‚Äî | Auth token for the registry. Falls back to the `NPM_TOKEN` env var when unset. Stored in `.npmrc` as `//<registry>/:_authToken=...` at publish time and never passed via argv. |
| `url_template` | string | ‚Äî | Override the download URL emitted into the postinstall script (templated). When unset, anodizer derives the URL from the release context. Only consulted in `postinstall` mode. |

## `partial`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `by` | string | ‚Äî | How to split builds: "os" (by OS, default) or "target" (by full triple). "os" groups all arch variants for the same OS into one split job. "target" gives each unique target triple its own split job.

The legacy `goos` spelling is accepted as a back-compat alias for `os` (folded at parse time, with a deprecation warning); imported configs keep loading. |

## `publishers`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `args` | list of string | ‚Äî | Arguments passed to the publish command (supports templates). |
| `artifact_types` | list of string | ‚Äî | Artifact type filter: only publish artifacts of these types (e.g., "archive", "binary"). |
| `checksum` | bool | ‚Äî | Include checksums in published artifacts. |
| `cmd` | string | ‚Äî | Command to invoke for publishing. |
| `dir` | string | ‚Äî | Working directory for the publisher command. |
| `env` | list of string | ‚Äî | Environment variables passed to the publish command. |
| `extra_files` | list of ExtraFileSpec | ‚Äî | Extra files to include in publishing (glob patterns with optional name override). |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build IDs filter: only publish artifacts from builds whose `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the publisher is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. The `customization/publishers/` `if:` field. Distinct from `skip:` (which expresses "always skip") and provides config-import parity. |
| `meta` | bool | ‚Äî | Include metadata artifacts in published artifacts. |
| `name` | string | ‚Äî | Human-readable name for this publisher (used in logs). |
| `signature` | bool | ‚Äî | Include signatures in published artifacts. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Template-conditional skip: if rendered result is `"true"`, skip this publisher. Accepts bool or template string (e.g. `"{{ if .IsSnapshot }}true{{ endif }}"`). Accepts the legacy `disable:` spelling via serde alias for back-compat. |
| `templated_extra_files` | list of TemplatedExtraFile | ‚Äî | Extra files whose contents are rendered through the template engine before publishing. Unlike `extra_files` which copy as-is, template variables like `{{ Tag }}` are expanded. |

## `release`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `discussion_category_name` | string | ‚Äî | GitHub Discussion category name for the release. |
| `draft` | bool | ‚Äî | When true, create the release as a draft (unpublished). |
| `extra_files` | list of ExtraFileSpec | ‚Äî | Extra files to upload to the release beyond build artifacts.

Paths / globs are resolved relative to the project root. `..` segments are accepted, so an entry like `../sibling/dist/*` will reach outside the project tree ‚Äî security-conscious users should keep the entries inside the repo or canonicalise them before invoking the release pipeline. |
| `footer` | ContentSource | ‚Äî | Text appended to the release body (inline string, from_file, or from_url). |
| `gitea` | ScmRepoConfig | ‚Äî | Gitea repository to release to (owner and name). |
| `github` | ScmRepoConfig | ‚Äî | GitHub repository to release to (owner and name). |
| `gitlab` | ScmRepoConfig | ‚Äî | GitLab repository to release to (owner and name). |
| `header` | ContentSource | ‚Äî | Text prepended to the release body (inline string, from_file, or from_url). |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Artifact IDs filter for uploads. |
| `include_meta` | bool | ‚Äî | Upload metadata.json and artifacts.json as release assets. |
| `make_latest` | object | ‚Äî | Mark release as latest: true, false, or "auto" (latest non-prerelease). |
| `mode` | string | ‚Äî | Release mode: "keep-existing", "append", "prepend", or "replace". |
| `name_template` | string | ‚Äî | Release title template (supports templates). |
| `prerelease` | object | ‚Äî | Mark release as pre-release: true, false, or "auto" (inferred from tag). |
| `provider` | ForceTokenKind | ‚Äî | Explicit publish target ‚Äî the SCM provider whose `release.<provider>` block the publisher uses. When set, overrides the implicit token-type fallback chain in [`crate::scm::resolve_token_type`].

Use this for **cross-platform publishing** pattern: source repo on one provider (e.g. GitLab) but releases land on another (e.g. GitHub). Without it, the publish target is inferred from which `*_TOKEN` env-var is set ‚Äî fine for single-provider setups but ambiguous when both tokens are available.

```yaml release: provider: github github: owner: my-org name: my-app ``` |
| `replace_existing_artifacts` | bool | ‚Äî | When true, replace existing release artifacts with the same name. |
| `replace_existing_draft` | bool | ‚Äî | When true, replace an existing draft release instead of failing. |
| `required` | bool | ‚Äî | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.

Default: `true` ‚Äî a failure here aborts the release. Set to `false` to log failures but continue. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip the release stage. Accepts bool or template string (e.g. `"{{ if IsSnapshot }}true{{ endif }}"` for conditional skip). Template strings are supported here. Accepts the legacy `disable:` spelling via serde alias for back-compat with imported configs (the legacy `disable:` spelling). |
| `skip_upload` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip uploading artifacts: true, false, or "auto" (skip for snapshots). Accepts bool or template string. |
| `tag` | string | ‚Äî | Override the release tag (template string). When set, this tag is used as the `tag_name` in the GitHub release API instead of the crate's `tag_template`. Useful in monorepo setups to strip a tag prefix (e.g. `"{{ Tag }}"` to publish `v1.0.0` instead of `myapp/v1.0.0`). A cross-platform publishing feature provided for free by anodizer. |
| `target_commitish` | string | ‚Äî | Target branch or SHA for the release tag. |
| `templated_extra_files` | list of TemplatedExtraFile | ‚Äî | Extra files whose contents are rendered through the template engine before upload. Unlike `extra_files` which copy as-is, template variables like `{{ Tag }}` are expanded.

Same path-traversal caveat as `extra_files`: `..` segments reach outside the project tree. |
| `upload_concurrency` | integer | ‚Äî | Maximum number of asset-upload requests in flight simultaneously.

GitHub's secondary rate-limit is triggered by burst traffic. Keeping this value low avoids tripping the limit even for releases with many artifacts. Default: 4. Override at runtime with `ANODIZER_GITHUB_UPLOAD_CONCURRENCY`. |
| `use_existing_draft` | bool | ‚Äî | Reuse an existing draft release instead of creating a new one. |

## `retry`
User-facing retry configuration block (`retry:` at config root).

All fields are optional in YAML; missing fields fall back to the defaults (10 attempts, 10s base delay, 5m cap).
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `attempts` | integer | `10` | Total attempts (including the first). Default `10`. Values < 1 are clamped up to 1 by the policy layer. |
| `delay` | HumanDuration | `10s` | Initial delay before the second attempt. Default `10s`. Subsequent delays grow exponentially (`delay √ó 2^(n-2)`) up to [`Self::max_delay`]. |
| `max_delay` | HumanDuration | `5m` | Upper bound on any individual sleep between attempts. Default `5m`. Without this cap, an exponential backoff with `delay=10s` would stretch attempt 9 to ~42 minutes. |

## `sboms`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `args` | list of string | ‚Äî | Command-line arguments (supports templates and $artifact, $document vars). |
| `artifacts` | string | ‚Äî | Which artifacts to catalog: "source", "archive", "binary", "package", "diskimage", "installer", "any" (default: "archive"). |
| `cmd` | string | ‚Äî | Command to run for SBOM generation (default: "syft"). |
| `documents` | list of string | ‚Äî | Output document path templates (supports templates). |
| `env` | list of string | ‚Äî | Environment variables to pass to the command, as `KEY=VALUE` strings. Order is preserved. Values are template-rendered before being set. |
