| `id` | string | ‚Äî | Unique identifier for this SBOM config (default: "default"). |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Filter by artifact IDs (ignored if artifacts="source"). |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip this SBOM config. Accepts bool or template string. Accepts the legacy `disable:` spelling via serde alias for back-compat. |

## `schemastore`
Top-level `schemastore:` block. Shared fields here are defaults for every entry in `schemas`; a per-entry field overrides them (cascade).
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `commit_author` | CommitAuthorConfig | ‚Äî | Commit author for the SchemaStore commit (defaults to git config). |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Tera condition; when it renders falsy the publisher is skipped. |
| `repository` | RepositoryConfig | ‚Äî | Fork of `SchemaStore/schemastore` to push branches to and open the PR from. |
| `schemas` | list of SchemaEntry | `[]` | The schema entries to register/refresh. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip the whole publisher. Alias: `disable`. |
| `versioned` | bool | ‚Äî | Default for `SchemaEntry::versioned`. |

## `signs`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `args` | list of string | ‚Äî | Arguments passed to the signing command (supports templates with ${artifact} and ${signature}). |
| `artifacts` | string | ‚Äî | Artifact types to sign: "all", "archive", "binary", "checksum", "package", "sbom" (default: "none"). |
| `certificate` | string | ‚Äî | Certificate file to embed in the signature (Cosign bundle signing). |
| `cmd` | string | ‚Äî | Signing command to invoke (default: "cosign" or "gpg"). |
| `env` | list of string | ‚Äî | Environment variables passed to the signing command. |
| `id` | string | ‚Äî | Unique identifier for this sign config. |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build IDs filter: only sign artifacts from builds whose `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional: skip this sign config if rendered result is "false" or empty. |
| `output` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Capture and log stdout/stderr of the signing command. Accepts bool or template string (e.g., "{{ IsSnapshot }}"). |
| `signature` | string | ‚Äî | Signature output filename template (supports templates). |
| `stdin` | string | ‚Äî | Content written to the signing command's stdin. |
| `stdin_file` | string | ‚Äî | Path to a file whose content is written to the signing command's stdin. |

## `snapshot`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `version_template` | string | ‚Äî | Version string template for snapshot builds (e.g., "{{ Commit }}-SNAPSHOT"). Accepts the deprecated `name_template:` alias (renamed to `version_template`): a non-empty `name_template` is folded into `version_template`. A deprecation warning is emitted at config-load time when the alias is hit (see `apply_snapshot_legacy_aliases`). |

## `source`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `enabled` | bool | ‚Äî | When true, generate a source code archive for the release. |
| `files` | list of SourceFileEntry | `[]` | Extra files to include in the source archive. Accepts strings (glob patterns) or objects with src/dst/info. |
| `format` | string | ‚Äî | Archive format for the source tarball: tar.gz, tgz, tar, or zip (default: tar.gz). |
| `name_template` | string | ‚Äî | Filename template for the source archive (supports templates). |
| `prefix_template` | string | ‚Äî | Prefix prepended to all paths inside the archive (supports templates). Defaults to name_template value. Use this to set a different prefix than the archive name. |

## `srpms`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `bins` | map | ‚Äî | Map of binary name ‚Üí install path declared in the spec's `%files` section. Each entry tells the generated `.spec` which installed file the package owns. When omitted, each binary produced by the build for this crate defaults to `%{_bindir}/<name>` (i.e. `/usr/bin/<name>`, the RPM-idiomatic location for a built binary). Provide this only to override the install path or to declare extra owned paths. Stored as a `BTreeMap` so the emitted `%files` section iterates in deterministic key order. |
| `build_host` | string | ‚Äî | Override the build host recorded in the RPM header. Useful for reproducible builds where the actual hostname leaks build-env detail. |
| `compression` | string | ‚Äî | Compression algorithm (gzip, xz, zstd, none). |
| `contents` | list of NfpmContent | ‚Äî | Additional contents to include in the source RPM. Shares the unified [`NfpmContent`] type with nFPM contents; SRPM-style `source:` / `destination:` / `type:` keys are accepted via serde aliases. |
| `description` | string | ‚Äî | Package description. |
| `docs` | list of string | ‚Äî | Documentation files to include. |
| `enabled` | bool | ‚Äî | Enable source RPM generation. Default: false. |
| `epoch` | string | ‚Äî | RPM epoch. |
| `file_name_template` | string | ‚Äî | Output filename template. |
| `group` | string | ‚Äî | RPM group. |
| `license` | string | ‚Äî | License identifier. |
| `license_file_name` | string | ‚Äî | License file name to include. |
| `maintainer` | string | ‚Äî | Package maintainer. |
| `package_name` | string | ‚Äî | Package name (default: project_name). |
| `packager` | string | ‚Äî | RPM packager field. |
| `posttrans` | string | ‚Äî | `%posttrans` scriptlet ‚Äî executed *after* all packages in the transaction have been installed. Path to a script file. |
| `prefixes` | list of string | ‚Äî | Filesystem prefixes the package may install to (RPM `Prefix:` tag). Each entry becomes one `Prefix:` directive ‚Äî relocatable RPMs need at least one prefix declared. |
| `prerelease` | string | ‚Äî | Prerelease suffix appended to the version (e.g. `rc1`, `beta2`). Prerelease component of the package version. |
| `pretrans` | string | ‚Äî | `%pretrans` scriptlet ‚Äî executed on the package transaction *before* any package in the transaction is installed. Path to a script file. |
| `section` | string | ‚Äî | RPM section. |
| `signature` | NfpmSignatureConfig | ‚Äî | RPM signature configuration. Shares the unified [`NfpmSignatureConfig`] type with nFPM. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip this config. Accepts bool or template string. |
| `spec_file` | string | ‚Äî | Path to the RPM spec file template. |
| `summary` | string | ‚Äî | Summary line. |
| `url` | string | ‚Äî | Homepage URL. |
| `vendor` | string | ‚Äî | Package vendor. |
| `version_metadata` | string | ‚Äî | Build metadata appended to the version (e.g. git commit hash). Version-metadata component of the package version. |

## `tag`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `branch_history` | string | ‚Äî | Branch history mode for determining the previous tag: "full" or "last". |
| `bump_minor_pre_major` | bool | ‚Äî | While the current major version is `0`, demote a conventional breaking change (`feat!:` / `BREAKING CHANGE`) from a major bump to a minor bump (e.g. `0.5.0` ‚Üí `0.6.0` instead of `1.0.0`). Honors the SemVer rule that anything may change in the `0.y.z` range. An explicit `#major`/`#minor` token, a `custom_tag`, or a manually-ahead `Cargo.toml` version always wins over this demotion. No-op once a real tag reaches `1.x`. Default false. |
| `bump_patch_for_minor_pre_major` | bool | ‚Äî | While the current major version is `0`, demote a conventional feature (`feat:`) from a minor bump to a patch bump (e.g. `0.5.0` ‚Üí `0.5.1` instead of `0.6.0`). Independent of `bump_minor_pre_major`. An explicit token / `custom_tag` / ahead `Cargo.toml` always wins. No-op at `1.x`. Default false. |
| `custom_tag` | string | ‚Äî | Custom version tag to use instead of auto-incrementing. |
| `default_bump` | string | ‚Äî | Default bump when a commit range carries no explicit `#` token and no conventional-commit marker: "major", "minor", "patch", or "none". Defaults to "none" ‚Äî a range of only chore/docs/style/refactor/test/ build/ci commits produces no release (the conventional-commit contract). Set "patch"/"minor" to cut a release on every range regardless of type. |
| `force_without_changes` | bool | ‚Äî | When true, create a new tag even if no commits have changed since the last tag. |
| `force_without_changes_pre` | bool | ‚Äî | Like force_without_changes but only for pre-release versions. |
| `git_api_tagging` | bool | ‚Äî | When true, use the GitHub/GitLab API for tagging instead of git CLI. |
| `initial_version` | string | ‚Äî | Version string to use when no previous tag exists (default: "0.1.0"). |
| `major_string_token` | string | ‚Äî | Conventional commit token triggering a major bump (default: "major"). |
| `minor_string_token` | string | ‚Äî | Conventional commit token triggering a minor bump (default: "minor" or "feat"). |
| `none_string_token` | string | ‚Äî | Conventional commit token suppressing a version bump entirely (default: "none"). |
| `patch_string_token` | string | ‚Äî | Conventional commit token triggering a patch bump (default: "patch" or "fix"). |
| `prerelease` | bool | ‚Äî | When true, apply a pre-release suffix to the generated version. |
| `prerelease_suffix` | string | ‚Äî | Suffix appended to pre-release versions (e.g., "beta"). |
| `push` | bool | ‚Äî | When true, `anodizer tag` also pushes the version-sync bump commit to the release branch (atomically with the tag), not just the tag. CLI `--push` / `--no-push` override this. Default false preserves the "push the tag, inspect the branch locally before pushing" workflow. |
| `release_branches` | list of string | ‚Äî | Branch name patterns (supports wildcards) that trigger releases (default: ["master", "main"]). |
| `skip_ci_on_bump` | bool | ‚Äî | Append `[skip ci]` to the version-sync bump commit subject.

Off by default. Only enable with a `workflow_run`-triggered release workflow: `[skip ci]` on the bump commit (which becomes the tag target) ALSO suppresses an `on: push: tags:` release trigger, so enabling this with a tag-push-triggered release silently skips the release. Leave off for the tag-push pattern; enable for the `workflow_run` pattern to skip the (already crate-gated, harmless) redundant CI re-run. |
| `tag_context` | string | ‚Äî | Source for determining the previous tag: "repo" (default) or "branch". |
| `tag_post_hooks` | list of HookEntry | ‚Äî | Commands to run after `anodizer tag` successfully creates and pushes the tag. Env and template vars same as `tag_pre_hooks`. |
| `tag_pre_hooks` | list of HookEntry | ‚Äî | Commands to run before `anodizer tag` creates the tag. Useful for updating lockfiles or committing sibling changes that must be part of the tagged commit. Env: `ANODIZER_CURRENT_TAG`, `ANODIZER_PREVIOUS_TAG` are set; template vars `{{ Tag }}`, `{{ PreviousTag }}`, `{{ Version }}`, `{{ PrefixedTag }}` are available. |
| `tag_prefix` | string | ‚Äî | Prefix prepended to version tags (e.g., "v" produces "v1.2.3"). |
| `verbose` | bool | ‚Äî | When true, print verbose tag calculation output. |

## `template_files`
Configuration for a template file that is rendered through the template engine and placed in the dist directory as a release artifact.

All rendered template files are uploaded to the release by default. Both `src` and `dst` paths support template rendering.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `dst` | string | ‚Äî | Destination filename, prefixed with the dist directory. Templates: allowed. |
| `id` | string | ‚Äî | Identifier for this template file entry (default: "default"). |
| `mode` | string | ‚Äî | File permissions in octal notation as a string, e.g. `"0755"` (default: `"0655"`). Parsed at runtime via `parse_octal_mode()` to avoid YAML interpreting as decimal. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip this entry when truthy. Accepts a literal bool or a Tera template that renders to `"true"`/`"false"` (e.g. `'{{ if eq .Os "windows" }}true{{ end }}'`). Mirrors the per-entry `skip:` pattern used by `ChangelogConfig`, `ChecksumConfig`, and the publishers. |
| `src` | string | ‚Äî | Source template file path. The file contents are rendered through the template engine. Templates: allowed (in path itself). |

## `uploads`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `checksum` | bool | ‚Äî | Include checksums in uploaded artifacts. |
| `checksum_header` | string | ‚Äî | Header name for the SHA256 checksum of the artifact. |
| `client_x509_cert` | string | ‚Äî | Path to PEM-encoded client X.509 certificate for mTLS. |
| `client_x509_key` | string | ‚Äî | Path to PEM-encoded client X.509 key for mTLS. |
| `custom_artifact_name` | bool | ‚Äî | When true, use the artifact name as-is (don't append to target URL). |
| `custom_headers` | map | ‚Äî | Custom HTTP headers (each value is template-expanded). |
| `extra_files` | list of ExtraFileSpec | ‚Äî | Extra files to include in uploading. |
| `extra_files_only` | bool | ‚Äî | Upload only extra files, skip normal artifacts. |
| `exts` | list of string | ‚Äî | File extension filter: only upload artifacts with these extensions. |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build IDs filter: only upload artifacts whose `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the upload is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. The `uploads[].if:` conditional gate. |
| `meta` | bool | ‚Äî | Include metadata artifacts in uploaded artifacts. |
| `method` | string | ‚Äî | HTTP method: PUT or POST (default: PUT). |
| `mode` | string | ‚Äî | Upload mode: "archive" (default) or "binary". |
| `name` | string | ‚Äî | Human-readable name for this upload config. |
| `password` | string | ‚Äî | Password for HTTP basic auth.

Strongly prefer `{{ Env.UPLOAD_PASSWORD }}` (or any other env-var template) over an in-config literal ‚Äî plaintext values here are NOT redacted from dry-run output and will land in `dist/config.yaml` when the pipeline runs with `--dry-run` / `--snapshot`. Resolution order: rendered `password` template ‚Üí env `UPLOAD_{NAME}_SECRET`. Password-resolution cascade. |
| `signature` | bool | ‚Äî | Include signatures in uploaded artifacts. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip condition template (if rendered to "true", skip this upload). |
| `target` | string | ‚Äî | Target URL template (supports template variables like {{ ProjectName }}, {{ Version }}). |
| `trusted_certificates` | string | ‚Äî | Path to PEM-encoded trusted CA certificates. |
| `username` | string | ‚Äî | Username for HTTP basic auth. Resolution order: rendered `username` template ‚Üí env `UPLOAD_{NAME}_USERNAME`. Set this to a literal value or a `{{ Env.X }}` template. |

## `upx`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `args` | list of string | `[]` | Extra arguments passed to UPX (e.g., ["-9", "--brute"]). |
| `binary` | string | `upx` | UPX executable path or name (default: "upx"). |
| `brute` | bool | ‚Äî | Use brute-force compression (--brute flag). Very slow but produces smallest output. |
| `compress` | string | ‚Äî | UPX compression level string (e.g., "1"-"9", "best"). Maps to `--compress` flag. |
| `enabled` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Whether to compress binaries with UPX. Accepts a bool or a template string that evaluates to a bool. |
| `id` | string | ‚Äî | Unique identifier for this UPX config. |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build IDs filter: only compress binaries from builds whose `id` is in this list. |
| `lzma` | bool | ‚Äî | Use LZMA compression (--lzma flag). |
| `required` | bool | `false` | When true, fail the build if UPX is not found. |
| `targets` | list of string | ‚Äî | Target triples to compress binaries for (empty means all targets). |

## `verify_release`
Top-level `verify_release:` block.

See the module-level docs for the verification lifecycle. The gate is a no-op unless `enabled: true`.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `assert_assets` | bool | `true` | Assert that every produced artifact has a matching uploaded asset on the published release. Default `true` (no extra config: anodizer already knows the produced set and can fetch the release's asset list). Independent of Docker and the network smoke-test. |
| `enabled` | bool | `false` | Whether to run the post-release verification gate at all. Default `false` ‚Äî the gate is opt-in because it needs the published release to already exist (it runs after publish) and, for install-smoke, a Docker daemon. |
| `glibc_ceiling` | string | ‚Äî | glibc version ceiling, e.g. `"2.36"`. When any glibc-linked `.deb` requires a glibc NEWER than this floor, the gate reports it and exits non-zero. `None` (the default) disables the libc check entirely. musl binaries have no glibc requirement and are skipped. |
| `install_smoke` | InstallSmokeConfig | ‚Äî | Per-package install smoke-test images. When `None`, smoke-testing is off. When present, each package type that produced an artifact is installed in its (configured or default) container and `<bin> --version` is run. |

## `workspaces`
A workspace represents an independent project root within a monorepo. Each workspace has its own crates, changelog, and release configuration, allowing independently-versioned components that aren't Cargo workspace members.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `after` | HooksConfig | ‚Äî | Hooks run after this workspace's pipeline completes. |
| `before` | HooksConfig | ‚Äî | Hooks run before this workspace's pipeline starts. |
| `binary_signs` | list of SignConfig | `[]` | Binary-specific signing configs (same shape as `signs` but only for binary artifacts). The `artifacts` field on each entry is constrained at parse time to `binary` / `none` (or omitted) ‚Äî a broader filter on `binary_signs` would silently match nothing because the loop only iterates Binary artifacts. Constraint lives in `deserialize_binary_signs`. |
| `changelog` | ChangelogConfig | ‚Äî | Changelog configuration for this workspace. |
| `crates` | list of CrateConfig | `[]` | Crates belonging to this workspace. |
| `env` | list of string | ‚Äî | Environment variables scoped to this workspace.

List of `KEY=VALUE` strings. Order is preserved. Values are template-rendered at pipeline startup. |
| `name` | string | ‚Äî | Workspace identifier used in logs and template variables. |
| `signs` | list of SignConfig | `[]` | Signing configurations for binaries, archives, and checksums. |
| `skip` | list of string | `[]` | Pipeline stages to skip when releasing this workspace. Stage names match the CLI `--skip` flag (e.g., `announce`, `publish`). |

## `crates[].dockers_v2`
Docker V2 configuration ‚Äî the canonical Docker build API.

Notable surface: - `images` + `tags` (cleaner separation than a single `image_templates` list) - `annotations` map for OCI annotations (`--annotation`) - `build_args` map for build-time variables - `skip` as a [`StringOrBool`] template for conditional opt-out - `sbom` as a [`StringOrBool`] ‚Äî when truthy, adds `--sbom=true` to buildx - `flags` for arbitrary extra `docker build` flags - `platforms` is the only target selector ‚Äî no per-arch field overrides
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `annotations` | map | ‚Äî | OCI annotations to apply via `--annotation key=value` flags. |
| `build_args` | map | ‚Äî | Build arguments passed as `--build-arg KEY=VALUE`.

Each value is template-expanded and forwarded verbatim to buildx (one argv token per pair, no shell tokenization). Prefer `{{ Env.VAR }}` over raw user-config strings for secrets ‚Äî buildx records build-args in image history by default, so plaintext values here propagate into the image metadata. |
| `dockerfile` | string | ‚Äî | Path to the Dockerfile relative to the project root. |
| `extra_files` | list of string | ‚Äî | Extra files to copy into the Docker build context. |
| `flags` | list of string | ‚Äî | Arbitrary extra flags passed to the docker build command. |
| `hooks` | BuildHooksConfig | ‚Äî | Pre/post hooks for this dockers_v2 config. Each hook accepts the same `cmd`/`dir`/`env`/`output` shape as build/archive hooks. `pre` hooks run after the staging directory is prepared but before `docker buildx build`; `post` hooks run after the image digest is captured. Hook commands, working directories, and env values are template-expanded; in addition to the standard template surface, hooks see:

- `{{ Images }}` ‚Äî list of `image:tag` references for this build. Iterate via `{% for img in Images %}{{ img }}{% endfor %}` to mirror a list exposure of the same field; `{{ Images \| join(sep=",") }}` reproduces a flat comma-separated string for legacy templates. - `{{ Dockerfile }}` ‚Äî path to the rendered Dockerfile - `{{ ContextDir }}` ‚Äî path to the buildx context staging directory - `{{ Digest }}` ‚Äî image manifest digest (post hooks only) - `{{ BaseImage }}` / `{{ BaseImageDigest }}` ‚Äî final-stage base image (the `BaseImage` / `BaseImageDigest` overlay) |
| `id` | string | ‚Äî | Unique identifier for this Docker V2 config. |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build IDs filter: only include binary artifacts whose metadata `id` is in this list. |
| `images` | list of string | `[]` | Base image names (e.g., ["ghcr.io/owner/app"]). Combined with `tags` to form full references. |
| `labels` | map | ‚Äî | OCI labels to apply to the image via `--label key=value` flags. |
| `platforms` | list of string | ‚Äî | Target platforms for multi-arch builds (e.g., ["linux/amd64", "linux/arm64"]). |
| `retry` | DockerRetryConfig | ‚Äî | Retry configuration for docker push operations. |
| `sbom` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | When truthy, adds `--sbom=true` to buildx. Supports templates. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | When truthy, skip this docker build entirely. Supports templates. Accepts the legacy `disable:` spelling via serde alias for back-compat. |
| `tags` | list of string | `[]` | Tag suffixes (e.g., ["latest", "{{ Version }}"]). Each image is tagged with each tag. |
| `use` | string | ‚Äî | Docker backend for build commands: `"buildx"` (default) or `"podman"`.

The default `"buildx"` invokes `docker buildx build` with the full set of BuildKit features (multi-platform, attestations, `--rewrite-timestamp`, SBOM, OCI exporter). Setting `use: podman` swaps the binary to `podman build` and disables every buildx-only flag ‚Äî anodizer rejects configs that mix `use: podman` with `sbom: true`, `--rewrite-timestamp`, `--provenance`, `--attest`, `--cache-from`, `--cache-to`, `--output`, or `--sbom` because plain podman does not recognise them.

**Linux-only.** The podman backend is restricted to Linux hosts. Configs setting `use: podman` on macOS or Windows fail at config-validation time with a clear error rather than blowing up later when `podman` is not on `PATH`. |

## `crates[].docker_manifests`
Deprecated: prefer `dockers_v2` (which produces multi-arch manifests via the `platforms:` field automatically). `DockerManifestConfig` is retained for back-compat with imported configs and for the niche case of stitching together manifest lists from images that were not built by `dockers_v2` in the same run.

The v1 docker / docker manifest pipes deprecated in favour of the v2 buildx flow. The rustdoc here is the load-bearing surface for the deprecation: it flows into the schemars-generated JSON Schema (consumed by IDEs / editor tooling) and rustdoc HTML, both of which are how downstream config authors discover that the v2 pipe is the preferred entry point.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `create_flags` | list of string | ‚Äî | Extra flags for `docker manifest create`. |
| `id` | string | ‚Äî | Unique identifier for this manifest config. |
| `image_templates` | list of string | `[]` | Image references to include in the manifest. |
| `name_template` | string | ‚Äî | Template for the manifest name, e.g. "ghcr.io/owner/app:{{ Version }}". |
| `push_flags` | list of string | ‚Äî | Extra flags for `docker manifest push`. |
| `retry` | DockerRetryConfig | ‚Äî | Retry configuration for manifest push (handles transient registry errors). |
| `skip_push` | object | ‚Äî | Skip push: true, false, or "auto" (skip for prereleases). |
| `use` | string | ‚Äî | Docker backend for manifest commands: `"docker"` (default) or `"podman"`. The `"podman"` backend is **Linux-only** (per Pro): configs on macOS or Windows fail at config-validation time with a clear error rather than blowing up later when `podman` is not on `PATH`. |

## `crates[].docker_digest`
Controls docker image digest file creation.

After each docker image push, a digest file (containing the sha256 digest) is written to the dist directory. This config controls whether that happens and how the files are named.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `name_template` | string | ‚Äî | Template for the digest artifact filename. Default: tag-based naming (e.g., "ghcr.io_owner_app_v1.0.0.digest"). |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | When truthy, disable docker digest artifact creation. Accepts the legacy `disable:` spelling via serde alias for back-compat. |

## `crates[].publish`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `aur` | AurConfig | ‚Äî | AUR (Arch User Repository) binary package publishing configuration. |
| `aur_source` | AurSourceConfig | ‚Äî | AUR source package publishing configuration (source-only PKGBUILD, not -bin). |
| `cargo` | CargoPublishConfig | ‚Äî | Publish to crates.io. Presence opts in; use `cargo: { skip: true }` to opt out. |
| `chocolatey` | ChocolateyConfig | ‚Äî | Chocolatey package publishing configuration. |
| `homebrew` | HomebrewConfig | ‚Äî | Homebrew formula publishing configuration. |
| `homebrew_cask` | HomebrewCaskConfig | ‚Äî | Homebrew Cask publishing configuration (macOS .app bundles).

Uses the unified `HomebrewCaskConfig` which carries all fields from both the per-crate cask config and the top-level `homebrew_casks:` config. |
| `krew` | KrewConfig | ‚Äî | Krew (kubectl plugin manager) manifest publishing configuration. |
| `nix` | NixConfig | ‚Äî | Nix derivation publishing configuration. |
| `scoop` | ScoopConfig | ‚Äî | Scoop manifest publishing configuration. |
| `winget` | WingetConfig | ‚Äî | WinGet manifest publishing configuration. |

## `crates[].publish.cargo`
`cargo publish` flag surface.

Presence under `publish:` opts the crate in; use `skip: true` (or a truthy template) to opt out. There is no `enabled` field ‚Äî presence is the on-switch.

Fields intentionally omitted because anodizer owns them: - `--package` / `--workspace` / `--exclude`: the top-level `crates[]` axis owns crate selection. - `--dry-run`: pipeline-level CLI ergonomics (`anodizer release --dry-run`). - `-v` / `-q` / `--color`: CLI ergonomics, not config. - `--config` / `-Z`: cargo CLI escape hatches; out of scope.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `all_features` | bool | ‚Äî | Activate every feature, including `default` (`--all-features`). |
| `allow_dirty` | bool | ‚Äî | Allow publishing with an uncommitted working tree (`--allow-dirty`). |
| `features` | list of string | ‚Äî | Crate features to activate (`--features`). |
| `frozen` | bool | ‚Äî | Both `--locked` and `--offline` (`--frozen`). |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the cargo publisher is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. Config key: the publisher's `if:`. |
| `index` | string | ‚Äî | Registry index URL (`--index`). |
| `index_timeout` | integer | ‚Äî | Seconds to wait for the crates.io sparse index to publish a crate before its dependents are pushed (anodizer-original ‚Äî no `cargo publish` equivalent). |
| `jobs` | integer | ‚Äî | Number of parallel compile jobs for verification (`--jobs`). |
| `keep_going` | bool | ‚Äî | Continue on errors when verifying multiple crates (`--keep-going`). |
| `locked` | bool | ‚Äî | Require an up-to-date `Cargo.lock` matching the resolver (`--locked`). |
| `manifest_path` | string | ‚Äî | Path to the crate's `Cargo.toml` (`--manifest-path`). |
| `no_default_features` | bool | ‚Äî | Disable the `default` feature set (`--no-default-features`). |
| `no_verify` | bool | ‚Äî | Skip the local `cargo build --release` verification step (`--no-verify`). |
| `offline` | bool | ‚Äî | Require offline resolution; never hit the network (`--offline`). |
| `registry` | string | ‚Äî | Alternate registry name from `~/.cargo/config.toml` (`--registry`). |
| `required` | bool | ‚Äî | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.

Default: `true` ‚Äî a failure here aborts the release. Set to `false` to log failures but continue. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip this publisher; supports template strings or bool. Truthy renders disable the publisher without removing the block. |
| `target` | string | ‚Äî | Build target triple for the verification step (`--target`). |
| `target_dir` | string | ‚Äî | Override the cargo target directory (`--target-dir`). |
| `wait_for_workspace_deps` | WaitForWorkspaceDepsConfig | ‚Äî | Pre-publish gate that polls crates.io for every workspace-internal dep of the crate being published, blocking until each is queryable at its expected version. Required for multi-tag-multi-crate workspaces (e.g. cfgd) where per-crate tags fire independent `Release.yml` runs that would otherwise race the sparse-index propagation.

Single-crate workspaces and lockstep-bumped monorepos (anodizer itself) leave this off ‚Äî there is no inter-tag race to gate on. |

## `crates[].publish.homebrew`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `amd64_variant` | string | ‚Äî | amd64 microarchitecture variant filter (e.g. "v1", "v2", "v3", "v4"). Only artifacts matching this variant are included. Default: "v1". |
| `arm_variant` | string | ‚Äî | ARM version filter (e.g. "6", "7"). Only artifacts matching this variant are included. |
| `cask` | HomebrewCaskConfig | ‚Äî | Homebrew Cask configuration (macOS .app bundles). |
| `caveats` | string | ‚Äî | Post-install user-facing notes shown by `brew info`. |
| `commit_author` | CommitAuthorConfig | ‚Äî | Commit author with optional signing. |
| `commit_msg_template` | string | ‚Äî | Custom commit message template. Rendered via Tera with the standard release template variables (`ProjectName`, `Tag`, `Version`, etc.). Default: `"Brew formula update for {{ ProjectName }} version {{ Tag }}"` (set in `crates/stage-publish/src/homebrew.rs::default_commit_msg_template`). |
| `conflicts` | list of HomebrewConflict | ‚Äî | Conflicting formula names with optional reason. |
| `custom_block` | string | ‚Äî | Custom Ruby code block inserted into the formula class body. |
| `custom_require` | string | ‚Äî | Ruby `require` statement for custom download strategies. |
| `dependencies` | list of HomebrewDependency | ‚Äî | Package dependencies (e.g. `openssl`, `libgit2`). |
| `description` | string | ‚Äî | Short description of the formula (shown in `brew info`). |
| `directory` | string | ‚Äî | Formula directory in the tap (e.g. "Formula"). |
| `download_strategy` | string | ‚Äî | Custom download strategy class name (e.g. `:using => GitHubPrivateRepositoryReleaseDownloadStrategy`). |
| `extra_install` | string | ‚Äî | Additional install commands appended after the main install block. |
| `homepage` | string | ‚Äî | Project homepage URL. Falls back to the GitHub release URL when unset. |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build IDs filter: only include artifacts whose `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the Homebrew publisher is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. Config key: `brews[].if:`. |
| `install` | string | ‚Äî | Ruby `install` block content for the formula. |
| `license` | string | ‚Äî | SPDX license identifier (e.g., "MIT", "Apache-2.0"). |
| `name` | string | ‚Äî | Override the formula name (default: crate name). |
| `plist` | string | ‚Äî | Launchd plist content for `brew services`. |
| `post_install` | string | ‚Äî | Post-install commands (separate `def post_install` block in formula). |
| `repository` | RepositoryConfig | ‚Äî | Unified repository config with branch, token, PR, git SSH support. (Replaces the legacy `tap: TapConfig` owner/name-only form.) |
| `required` | bool | ‚Äî | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.

Default: `false` ‚Äî a failure here is logged but does not abort the release. Set to `true` to fail the release on any error. |
| `service` | string | ‚Äî | Homebrew service block content (alternative to plist). |
| `skip_upload` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip publishing the formula.  `"true"` always skips; `"auto"` skips for prerelease versions. Accepts bool or template string. |
| `test` | string | ‚Äî | Ruby `test` block content for the formula (run by `brew test`). |
| `url_headers` | list of string | ‚Äî | HTTP headers to include in download requests (e.g. for private repos). |
| `url_template` | string | ‚Äî | Custom URL template for download URLs (overrides release URL). |

## `crates[].publish.homebrew_cask`
Unified Homebrew Cask configuration.

Used at both call-sites: - `homebrew_casks:` ‚Äî top-level array; carries `repository`, `commit_author`, `directory`, `ids`, `url`, structured `uninstall`/`zap`, etc. - `crates[].publish.homebrew_cask:` ‚Äî per-crate override; same shape, with `url_template` as the simpler URL alternative.

Fields from both original types are present; any field may be `None` at either call-site. The union avoids a two-type bifurcation while keeping both axes.
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `alternative_names` | list of string | ‚Äî | Alternative cask names (aliases). |
| `app` | string | ‚Äî | macOS .app bundle name (e.g. "MyApp.app"). |
| `binaries` | list of HomebrewCaskBinary | ‚Äî | Binary stubs to create in /usr/local/bin.

Each entry is either a bare string (`"my-cli"` ‚Üí emits `binary "my-cli"`) or a structured `{ name, target }` object (`{ name: "my-cli", target: "mycli" }` ‚Üí emits `binary "my-cli", target: "mycli"`). The `target:` form mirrors the Homebrew Ruby cask DSL for binary renames ‚Äî without it, a wrapped binary installs at the wrong path. Cask binary entry. |
| `binary` | string | ‚Äî | Deprecated singular spelling of [`Self::binaries`]. The upstream replaced `binary: foo` with `binaries: [foo]`; this field captures the legacy spelling so imported configs keep parsing. [`apply_homebrew_cask_legacy_singulars`](super::super::apply_homebrew_cask_legacy_singulars) folds the value into [`Self::binaries`] at config-load time and emits a one-time deprecation warning per occurrence. The field is excluded from serialization so a round-tripped config emits only the canonical plural form. |
| `caveats` | string | ‚Äî | Custom caveats shown after install. |
| `commit_author` | CommitAuthorConfig | ‚Äî | Commit author with optional signing. |
| `commit_msg_template` | string | ‚Äî | Custom commit message template. Default: "Brew cask update for {{ ProjectName }} version {{ Tag }}" |
| `completions` | HomebrewCaskCompletions | ‚Äî | Shell completion definitions. |
| `conflicts` | list of HomebrewCaskConflictEntry | ‚Äî | Conflicting casks or formulae. |
| `custom_block` | string | ‚Äî | Arbitrary Ruby code inserted into the cask block. |
| `dependencies` | list of HomebrewCaskDependencyEntry | ‚Äî | Cask dependencies (other casks or formulae). |
| `description` | string | ‚Äî | Cask description. |
| `directory` | string | ‚Äî | Subdirectory in the tap repo for cask placement (default: "Casks"). |
| `generate_completions_from_executable` | HomebrewCaskGeneratedCompletions | ‚Äî | Auto-generate shell completions from an executable. |
| `homepage` | string | ‚Äî | Project homepage URL. |
| `hooks` | HomebrewCaskHooks | ‚Äî | Pre/post install/uninstall hooks. |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build IDs filter: only include artifacts from builds whose `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the Homebrew Cask config is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. Config key: `homebrew_casks[].if:`. |
| `license` | string | ‚Äî | License identifier (SPDX). |
| `manpage` | string | ‚Äî | Deprecated singular spelling of [`Self::manpages`]. The upstream replaced `manpage: foo.1` with `manpages: [foo.1]`; this field captures the legacy spelling so imported configs keep parsing. [`apply_homebrew_cask_legacy_singulars`](super::super::apply_homebrew_cask_legacy_singulars) folds the value into [`Self::manpages`] at config-load time and emits a one-time deprecation warning per occurrence. The field is excluded from serialization so a round-tripped config emits only the canonical plural form. |
| `manpages` | list of string | ‚Äî | Manual page references to install. |
| `name` | string | ‚Äî | Cask name (default: crate / project name). |
| `repository` | RepositoryConfig | ‚Äî | Unified repository config for the Homebrew tap. |
| `required` | bool | ‚Äî | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.

Default: `false` ‚Äî a failure here is logged but does not abort the release. Set to `true` to fail the release on any error. |
| `service` | string | ‚Äî | Homebrew service definition. |
| `skip_upload` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip publishing the cask. `"true"` always skips; `"auto"` skips for prerelease versions. Accepts bool or template string. |
| `uninstall` | HomebrewCaskUninstall | ‚Äî | Structured uninstall stanza configuration. |
| `update_existing_pr` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | When true, force-push the updated cask file to the existing PR branch when a PR for the same head branch already exists. The PR content is updated in place rather than creating a duplicate. When false (default), the push is skipped and a warning is emitted so the operator sees that the publisher did not update the PR. |
| `url` | HomebrewCaskURL | ‚Äî | Structured download URL configuration (top-level axis). |
| `url_template` | string | ‚Äî | Simple URL template for the .dmg/.zip download (per-crate shorthand).

Cannot be combined with `url.template:` ‚Äî set one or the other. If both are present, config validation rejects the config at parse time. Use `url:` for the structured form (verified domain, custom headers, etc.) or `url_template:` for a bare string shorthand ‚Äî never both simultaneously. |
| `zap` | HomebrewCaskUninstall | ‚Äî | Deep uninstall (zap) stanza configuration. |

## `crates[].publish.scoop`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `amd64_variant` | string | ‚Äî | amd64 microarchitecture variant filter (e.g. "v1", "v2", "v3", "v4"). Only artifacts matching this variant are included. Default: "v1". |
| `commit_author` | CommitAuthorConfig | ‚Äî | Commit author with optional signing. |
| `commit_msg_template` | string | ‚Äî | Custom commit message template. |
| `depends` | list of string | ‚Äî | Application dependencies (other Scoop packages). |
| `description` | string | ‚Äî | Short description of the package (shown in `scoop info`). |
| `directory` | string | ‚Äî | Subdirectory in the bucket repo for manifest placement. |
| `homepage` | string | ‚Äî | Project homepage URL. Falls back to the GitHub-derived URL when unset. |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build IDs filter: only include artifacts whose `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the Scoop publisher is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. Config key: `scoop[].if:`. |
| `license` | string | ‚Äî | SPDX license identifier (e.g., "MIT", "Apache-2.0"). |
| `name` | string | ‚Äî | Override the manifest name (default: crate name). |
| `persist` | list of string | ‚Äî | Data paths persisted between Scoop updates. |
| `post_install` | list of string | ‚Äî | Commands to run after installation. |
| `pre_install` | list of string | ‚Äî | Commands to run before installation. |
| `repository` | RepositoryConfig | ‚Äî | Unified repository config with branch, token, PR, git SSH support. (Replaces the legacy `bucket: BucketConfig` owner/name-only form.) |
| `required` | bool | ‚Äî | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.

Default: `false` ‚Äî a failure here is logged but does not abort the release. Set to `true` to fail the release on any error. |
| `shortcuts` | list of list of string | ‚Äî | Start menu shortcuts as `[executable, label]` pairs. |
| `skip_upload` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip publishing the manifest.  `"true"` always skips; `"auto"` skips for prerelease versions. Accepts bool or template string. |
| `url_template` | string | ‚Äî | Custom URL template for download URLs (overrides release URL). |
| `use` | string | ‚Äî | Artifact selection: "archive" (default), "msi", or "nsis". |

## `crates[].publish.chocolatey`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `amd64_variant` | string | ‚Äî | amd64 microarchitecture variant filter (e.g. "v1", "v2", "v3", "v4"). Only artifacts matching this variant are included. Default: "v1". |
| `api_key` | string | ‚Äî | Chocolatey API key for `choco push`. Falls back to `CHOCOLATEY_API_KEY` env var. |
| `authors` | string | ‚Äî | Package author(s) displayed in the Chocolatey gallery. |
| `bug_tracker_url` | string | ‚Äî | Bug tracker URL. |
| `copyright` | string | ‚Äî | Copyright notice. |
| `dependencies` | list of ChocolateyDependency | ‚Äî | Package dependencies with optional version constraints. |
| `description` | string | ‚Äî | Package description (supports markdown). |
| `docs_url` | string | ‚Äî | Documentation URL. |
| `icon_url` | string | ‚Äî | URL to the package icon image shown in the Chocolatey gallery. |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build IDs filter: only include artifacts whose `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the Chocolatey publisher is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. Config key: `chocolateys[].if:`. |
| `license` | string | ‚Äî | SPDX license identifier (e.g., "MIT", "Apache-2.0"). |
| `license_url` | string | ‚Äî | Optional explicit license URL. Falls back to `https://opensource.org/licenses/<license>` when not set. |
| `name` | string | ‚Äî | Override the package name (default: crate name). |
| `owners` | string | ‚Äî | Package owners (Chocolatey gallery user). |
| `package_source_url` | string | ‚Äî | URL shown as the package source in the Chocolatey gallery. |
| `post_publish_poll` | PostPublishPollConfig | ‚Äî | Post-publish moderation-queue polling settings. Polling is disabled by default ‚Äî Chocolatey's community moderation queue routinely takes hours to days, and blocking a CI workflow on that wait is wrong. Opt in per-publisher with `post_publish_poll: { enabled: true }` when running locally and willing to wait, or disable globally via `--no-post-publish-poll`. |
| `project_source_url` | string | ‚Äî | Source code project URL. |
| `project_url` | string | ‚Äî | Project homepage URL. |
| `release_notes` | string | ‚Äî | Release notes for this version. |
| `repository` | RepositoryConfig | ‚Äî | Unified project repo config (owner/name). Used to derive `<projectUrl>` (the Chocolatey gallery link) and download URLs. `<projectUrl>` resolves through `project_url:` (if set) ‚Üí derived `https://github.com/{repository.owner}/{repository.name}`. |
| `republish_in_moderation` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | When true, re-push the nupkg even when a version is already in the community moderation queue (PackageStatus=Submitted). Chocolatey's API accepts re-pushes of in-moderation versions; the new nupkg replaces the queued one. When false (default), the push is skipped and a warning is emitted so the operator sees that the publisher did not push. |
| `require_license_acceptance` | bool | ‚Äî | Require license acceptance before install. |
| `required` | bool | ‚Äî | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.

Default: `false` ‚Äî a failure here is logged but does not abort the release. Set to `true` to fail the release on any error. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip pushing to the Chocolatey community repository. Bool, string, or template expression (e.g. `"{{ IsSnapshot }}"`). Accepts the legacy `skip_publish:` spelling for back-compat with configs; canonical name is `skip:` to align with every other publisher. |
| `source_repo` | string | ‚Äî | Push source URL (default: "https://push.chocolatey.org/"). |
| `summary` | string | ‚Äî | Short summary of the package. |
| `tags` | list of string | ‚Äî | Tags for the Chocolatey gallery (joined with single spaces in the emitted nuspec). Always a typed list ‚Äî the legacy space-separated-string form was dropped now for IDE-completion friendliness and to remove whitespace ambiguity. |
| `title` | string | ‚Äî | Package title (default: project name). |
| `url_template` | string | ‚Äî | Custom URL template for download URLs (overrides release URL). |
| `use` | string | ‚Äî | Artifact selection: "archive" (default), "msi", or "nsis". |

## `crates[].publish.winget`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `amd64_variant` | string | ‚Äî | amd64 microarchitecture variant filter (e.g. "v1", "v2", "v3", "v4"). Only artifacts matching this variant are included. Default: "v1". |
| `author` | string | ‚Äî | Author name. |
| `commit_author` | CommitAuthorConfig | ‚Äî | Commit author with optional signing. |
| `commit_msg_template` | string | ‚Äî | Custom commit message template. |
| `copyright` | string | ‚Äî | Copyright notice. |
| `copyright_url` | string | ‚Äî | Copyright URL. |
| `dependencies` | list of WingetDependency | ‚Äî | Package dependencies. |
| `description` | string | ‚Äî | Full package description displayed in the WinGet gallery. |
| `homepage` | string | ‚Äî | Project homepage URL. |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build IDs filter: only include artifacts whose `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the WinGet publisher is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. Config key: `winget[].if:`. |
| `installation_notes` | string | ‚Äî | Post-install notes shown to the user. |
| `license` | string | ‚Äî | License identifier (required, e.g. "MIT"). |
| `license_url` | string | ‚Äî | License URL. |
| `name` | string | ‚Äî | Override the package name (default: crate name). |
| `package_identifier` | string | ‚Äî | WinGet package identifier (e.g. "Publisher.AppName"). Auto-generated if empty. |
| `package_name` | string | ‚Äî | Package name as displayed (default: same as name). |
| `path` | string | ‚Äî | Manifest file path (auto-generated if empty from publisher/name/version). |
| `post_publish_poll` | PostPublishPollConfig | ‚Äî | Post-publish PR-validation polling settings. Polling is disabled by default ‚Äî winget-pkgs PR validation routinely takes hours to days, and blocking a CI workflow on that wait is wrong. Opt in per-publisher with `post_publish_poll: { enabled: true }` when running locally and willing to wait, or disable globally via `--no-post-publish-poll`. |
| `privacy_url` | string | ‚Äî | Privacy policy URL. |
| `product_code` | string | ‚Äî | Product code for the installer (used in Add/Remove Programs). |
| `publisher` | string | ‚Äî | Publisher name (required). |
| `publisher_support_url` | string | ‚Äî | Publisher support URL. |
| `publisher_url` | string | ‚Äî | Publisher homepage URL shown in the WinGet manifest. |
| `release_notes` | string | ‚Äî | Release notes for this version. |
| `release_notes_url` | string | ‚Äî | URL to full release notes. |
| `repository` | RepositoryConfig | ‚Äî | Unified repository config with branch, token, PR, git SSH support. (Replaces the legacy `manifests_repo: WingetManifestsRepoConfig`.) |
| `required` | bool | ‚Äî | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.

Default: `false` ‚Äî a failure here is logged but does not abort the release. Set to `true` to fail the release on any error. |
| `short_description` | string | ‚Äî | Short description (required, max 256 chars). |
| `skip_upload` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip publishing. `"true"` always skips; `"auto"` skips for prereleases. Accepts bool or template string. |
| `tags` | list of string | ‚Äî | Tags for package discovery (lowercased, spaces‚Üíhyphens). |
| `update_existing_pr` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | When true, force-push the updated manifest to the existing PR branch when a PR for the same head branch already exists. The PR content is updated in place rather than creating a duplicate. When false (default), the push is skipped and a warning is emitted so the operator sees that the publisher did not update the PR. |
| `url_template` | string | ‚Äî | Custom URL template for download URLs (overrides release URL). |
| `use` | string | ‚Äî | Artifact selection: "archive" (default), "msi", or "nsis". |

## `crates[].publish.aur`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `amd64_variant` | string | ‚Äî | amd64 microarchitecture variant filter (e.g. "v1", "v2", "v3", "v4"). Only artifacts matching this variant are included. Default: "v1". |
| `backup` | list of string | ‚Äî | List of config files to preserve on upgrade (relative to `/`). |
| `commit_author` | CommitAuthorConfig | ‚Äî | Commit author with optional signing. |
| `commit_msg_template` | string | ‚Äî | Custom commit message template. Default: "Update to {{ version }}". |
| `conflicts` | list of string | ‚Äî | Packages this PKGBUILD conflicts with. |
| `contributors` | list of string | ‚Äî | Contributors listed in PKGBUILD comments. |
| `depends` | list of string | ‚Äî | Runtime dependencies required by this package. |
| `description` | string | ‚Äî | Short description of the package for PKGBUILD. |
| `directory` | string | ‚Äî | Subdirectory in the git repo for committed files. |
| `git_ssh_command` | string | ‚Äî | Custom SSH command for git operations. |
| `git_url` | string | ‚Äî | AUR SSH git URL override. Defaults to `ssh://aur@aur.archlinux.org/<package>.git`, derived from the resolved package name; set this only for a non-standard endpoint. |
| `homepage` | string | ‚Äî | Project homepage URL. |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build IDs filter: only include artifacts whose `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the AUR publisher is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. The `aurs[].if:` conditional gate. |
| `install` | string | ‚Äî | Content for a .install file (post-install/pre-remove scripts). |
| `license` | string | ‚Äî | SPDX license identifier (e.g., "MIT", "Apache-2.0"). |
| `maintainers` | list of string | ‚Äî | PKGBUILD maintainer entries (e.g., "Name <email@example.com>"). |
| `name` | string | ‚Äî | Override the package name (default: crate name + "-bin"). |
| `optdepends` | list of string | ‚Äî | Optional dependencies with descriptions (e.g., "fzf: fuzzy finder support"). |
| `package` | string | ‚Äî | Custom PKGBUILD `package()` function body. |
| `private_key` | string | ‚Äî | Path to SSH private key file. |
| `provides` | list of string | ‚Äî | Packages this PKGBUILD provides (virtual package names). |
| `rel` | string | ‚Äî | Package release number (default: "1"). |
| `replaces` | list of string | ‚Äî | Packages this PKGBUILD replaces (for upgrade paths from old package names). |
| `required` | bool | ‚Äî | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.

Default: `false` ‚Äî a failure here is logged but does not abort the release. Set to `true` to fail the release on any error. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip this AUR config. Accepts bool or template string (e.g. `"{{ if .IsSnapshot }}true{{ endif }}"` for conditional skip). Accepts the legacy `disable:` spelling via serde alias for back-compat. |
| `skip_upload` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip publishing. `"true"` always skips; `"auto"` skips for prereleases. Accepts bool or template string. |
| `url_template` | string | ‚Äî | Custom URL template for download URLs (overrides release URL). |

## `crates[].publish.aur_source`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `amd64_variant` | Amd64Variant | ‚Äî | `x86_64` micro-architecture variant ‚Äî `v1` (baseline), `v2`, `v3` (AVX2), or `v4`. Constrained to a typed enum because AUR source pkgs build from the upstream tarball (no binary artifacts to filter), so the value's only role is as the `Amd64` template var consumed by `prepare:` / `build:` / `package:` script bodies ‚Äî typos must fail at parse time, not silently render an invalid string into the PKGBUILD. When unset, defaults to `v1` at template-render time. |
| `arches` | list of string | ‚Äî | Explicit architecture list (default: auto-detect from artifacts). |
| `backup` | list of string | ‚Äî | Backup files to preserve on upgrade. |
| `build` | string | ‚Äî | Custom `build()` function body for PKGBUILD. |
| `commit_author` | CommitAuthorConfig | ‚Äî | Commit author with optional signing. |
| `commit_msg_template` | string | ‚Äî | Custom commit message template. |
| `conflicts` | list of string | ‚Äî | Packages this PKGBUILD conflicts with. |
| `contributors` | list of string | ‚Äî | Contributors listed in PKGBUILD comments. |
| `depends` | list of string | ‚Äî | Runtime dependencies. |
| `description` | string | ‚Äî | Short description of the package. |
| `directory` | string | ‚Äî | Subdirectory in the git repo for committed files. |
| `git_ssh_command` | string | ‚Äî | Custom SSH command for git operations. |
| `git_url` | string | ‚Äî | AUR SSH git URL override. Defaults to `ssh://aur@aur.archlinux.org/<package>.git`, derived from the resolved package name; set this only for a non-standard endpoint. |
| `homepage` | string | ‚Äî | Project homepage URL. |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build IDs filter. |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the AUR source config is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. The `aur_sources[].if:`. |
| `install` | string | ‚Äî | Content for a .install file (post-install/pre-remove scripts). |
| `license` | string | ‚Äî | SPDX license identifier. |
| `maintainers` | list of string | ‚Äî | PKGBUILD maintainer entries. |
| `makedepends` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build-time dependencies (source packages need these). |
| `name` | string | ‚Äî | Override the package name (default: crate name, no -bin suffix). |
| `optdepends` | list of string | ‚Äî | Optional dependencies. |
| `package` | string | ‚Äî | Custom `package()` function body for PKGBUILD. |
| `prepare` | string | ‚Äî | Custom `prepare()` function body for PKGBUILD. |
| `private_key` | string | ‚Äî | Path to SSH private key file. |
| `provides` | list of string | ‚Äî | Packages this PKGBUILD provides. |
| `rel` | string | ‚Äî | Package release number (default: "1"). |
| `required` | bool | ‚Äî | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.

Default: `false` ‚Äî a failure here is logged but does not abort the release. Set to `true` to fail the release on any error. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip this config. Accepts the legacy `disable:` spelling via serde alias for back-compat. |
| `skip_upload` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip publishing. `"true"` always skips; `"auto"` skips for prereleases. |
| `url_template` | string | ‚Äî | Custom URL template for download URLs. |

## `crates[].publish.krew`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `amd64_variant` | string | ‚Äî | amd64 microarchitecture variant filter (e.g. "v1", "v2", "v3", "v4"). Only artifacts matching this variant are included. Default: "v1". |
| `arm_variant` | string | ‚Äî | ARM version filter (e.g. "6", "7"). Only artifacts matching this variant are included. |
| `caveats` | string | ‚Äî | Post-install message shown to the user. |
| `commit_author` | CommitAuthorConfig | ‚Äî | Commit author with optional signing. |
| `commit_msg_template` | string | ‚Äî | Custom commit message template. |
| `description` | string | ‚Äî | Full description of the kubectl plugin. |
| `homepage` | string | ‚Äî | Project homepage URL for the plugin. |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build IDs filter: only include artifacts whose `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the Krew publisher is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. Config key: `krews[].if:`. |
| `mode` | KrewMode | ‚Äî | Which krew-index submission path to take.

- `auto` (default): probe whether the plugin already exists in `kubernetes-sigs/krew-index`. Already present ‚Üí `bot` (the hosted krew-release-bot opens the version-bump PR server-side); definitively absent ‚Üí `pr-direct` (anodizer opens the initial fork PR). A probe that can't reach a definitive answer (rate-limit, network error) hard-errors rather than guessing, so a transient blip never routes an existing plugin into a maintainer-hostile fork PR. - `bot`: always POST to the krew-release-bot webhook. Use when the plugin is known to be in krew-index and you want to skip the membership probe entirely. - `pr-direct`: always open a fork PR against krew-index. Use for the initial submission, or a self-hosted krew-index mirror the hosted bot can't reach. |
| `name` | string | ‚Äî | Override the plugin name (default: crate name). |
| `repository` | RepositoryConfig | ‚Äî | Unified repository config with branch, token, PR, git SSH support. (Replaces the legacy `manifests_repo:` / `upstream_repo:` form.) The upstream PR target is derived from `repository.pull_request.base` when set, falling back to the canonical kubernetes-sigs/krew-index. |
| `required` | bool | ‚Äî | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.

Default: `false` ‚Äî a failure here is logged but does not abort the release. Set to `true` to fail the release on any error. |
| `short_description` | string | ‚Äî | One-line summary of the kubectl plugin (max 255 chars). |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip this Krew config. Accepts bool or template string (e.g. `"{{ if .IsSnapshot }}true{{ endif }}"` for conditional skip). Distinct from `skip_upload` so users can opt out of generating the manifest entirely (common when a project is not a kubectl plugin and has no krew channel). |
| `skip_upload` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip publishing. `"true"` always skips; `"auto"` skips for prereleases. Accepts bool or template string. |
| `update_existing_pr` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | When true, force-push the updated plugin manifest to the existing PR branch when a PR for the same head branch already exists. The PR content is updated in place rather than creating a duplicate. When false (default), the push is skipped and a warning is emitted so the operator sees that the publisher did not update the PR. |
| `url_template` | string | ‚Äî | Custom URL template for download URLs (overrides release URL). |

## `crates[].publish.nix`
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `amd64_variant` | string | ‚Äî | amd64 microarchitecture variant filter (e.g. "v1", "v2", "v3", "v4"). Only artifacts matching this variant are included. Default: "v1". |
| `commit_author` | CommitAuthorConfig | ‚Äî | Commit author with optional signing. |
| `commit_msg_template` | string | ‚Äî | Custom commit message template. |
| `dependencies` | list of NixDependency | ‚Äî | Nix package dependencies with optional OS filtering. |
| `description` | string | ‚Äî | Short description of the Nix derivation. |
| `extra_install` | string | ‚Äî | Additional install commands appended after the main install. |
| `formatter` | string | ‚Äî | Nix formatter to run on the generated file: "alejandra" or "nixfmt". |
| `homepage` | string | ‚Äî | Project homepage URL. |
| `ids` | list of string | ‚Äî | Build IDs filter: only include artifacts whose `id` is in this list. |
| `if` | string | ‚Äî | Template-conditional gate: when the rendered result is falsy (`"false"` / `"0"` / `"no"` / empty), the Nix publisher is skipped. Render failure hard-errors. Config key: `nix[].if:`. |
| `install` | string | ‚Äî | Custom install commands (replaces auto-generated binary install). |
| `license` | string | ‚Äî | Nix license identifier (e.g. "mit", "asl20"). Validated against known licenses. |
| `main_program` | string | ‚Äî | Value for `meta.mainProgram` in the generated Nix derivation. When set, the rendered derivation includes `mainProgram = "<value>";` inside the `meta` block, telling Nix which binary `nix run` should execute when the derivation contains multiple executables. Templated: supports `{{ Version }}` etc. Omitted when unset. |
| `name` | string | ‚Äî | Override the derivation name (default: crate name). |
| `path` | string | ‚Äî | Path for the .nix file in the repository (default: `pkgs/<name>/default.nix`). |
| `post_install` | string | ‚Äî | Post-install commands (postInstall phase). |
| `repository` | RepositoryConfig | ‚Äî | Unified repository config with branch, token, PR, git SSH support. |
| `required` | bool | ‚Äî | Override whether this publisher failing should fail the overall release.

Default: `false` ‚Äî a failure here is logged but does not abort the release. Set to `true` to fail the release on any error. |
| `skip` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip this Nix config. Accepts bool or template string (e.g. `"{{ if .IsSnapshot }}true{{ endif }}"` for conditional skip). Distinct from `skip_upload` so users can model both intents ‚Äî disable means "don't generate at all", skip_upload means "generate but don't push". Without this field, `nix: { skip: true }` was silently dropped by the serde unknown-field default. |
| `skip_upload` | StringOrBool | ‚Äî | Skip publishing. `"true"` always skips; `"auto"` skips for prereleases. Accepts bool or template string. |
| `url_template` | string | ‚Äî | Custom URL template for download URLs (overrides release URL). |
